what I love most about this playdough is that it’s non-toxic. my 2-year-old is a bit of a chewer, and I feel safe letting her play with it. however, it does crumble easily, which leaves a mess everywhere. overall, I’d say it’s good for supervised play!

the sculpting tools that came with this kit are surprisingly useful. they let me get into the tiny details which I didn't think would matter much. however, the clay itself isn't as pliable as I had hoped. it takes a little extra effort to mold things just right, but it’s still better than some others I've tried.

my kids had a blast with this modeling clay! they’ve made everything from dinosaurs to flowers. I love that it comes in a bunch of colors, but after several days of play, some of them got a bit hard. I guess that’s expected with playdough, but a bit disappointing.

this clay is awesome for projects! I’ve used it for about 3 months now and it’s held up well. however, it can be quite messy and does stain some surfaces if you’re not careful. overall, it works well for my art classes but I just recommend using it on a mat or some protective layer.

must say, I was initially skeptical about this playdough because of some mixed reviews, but it’s been pretty good for my 4-year-old. he enjoys playing with it, but it does harden quicker than I thought. keep it sealed tight!

I bought this sculpting clay for my pottery class and honestly, it’s decent but not amazing. it holds shape nicely, but I noticed it doesn’t blend as well as I expected. I’m hoping for a more professional feel as I improve my skills!

the colors in this modeling clay are super bright and vibrant! my kids went wild with it. however, they did find certain colors to be more crumbly than others, which was a bit frustrating while creating. overall, it's been a fun addition to our craft box.

I really want to love this playdough, but it dries out faster than I anticipated. I store it properly, but still found some of it hard after just a week. when it's fresh, it works great, so just keep an eye on storage!

got this for my niece's birthday and she was thrilled! the variety of tools that came with it was an unexpected surprise. but I noticed the clay itself is a bit harder than expected, which made it challenging for her little hands. still, happy with the purchase overall!

the modeling clay is perfect for my 8-year-old who loves crafting. it's soft and easy to mold, but I did notice it can stain clothing if not careful. she had a fun time making a ton of little figures, so I’m pleased enough with it!

I thought this playdough would be a hit for my daycare, and while the kids loved it, the texture was too sticky for my liking. it gets all over everything! still, they really enjoyed playing with it and it washed off surfaces easily, which is a plus.

the sculpting tools included with this set are great, but the clay itself is a letdown. it's hard to shape without it crumbling. I’ll probably stick to the tools and find a different clay that lasts longer. a bit disappointed but still having fun experimenting!

after spending around $25 on this modeling clay kit, I did have higher expectations. it’s been fun creating with my kids, but I wish the colors would stay fresh longer. they do dry out a bit too fast for my liking. still, we make it work!

I purchased this playdough for my son’s birthday party and it was a hit! the kids all enjoyed it, but I will say it took a while to clean up after. it left little bits everywhere! be prepared for the mess, but it kept them entertained for hours!

was looking for some good quality modeling clay for a school project, and this fit the bill! it wasn’t too pricey and worked pretty well. my only complaint is that it was a bit tougher to sculpt than expected, but I managed to get my project done!

I do like this sculpting clay, but it does seem to dry out quickly. I tried to keep it in an airtight container, but after a week, I found some pieces hard. the colors are nice, though, and the kids had a lot of fun making things with it!

the packaging is super cute, which attracted my little ones, but I was a bit disappointed with the clay itself. it’s fine for casual use, but not as pliable as I expected for serious sculpting. great for playtime, but not for my detailed projects.

this modeling clay is a decent option for kids, but I wish they offered a larger quantity for the price. we went through it pretty fast, and with multiple kids using it, it just didn’t last long enough to keep everyone entertained.

the colors are vibrant and the kids had a great time creating with this clay! our only issue was that it dried out a bit too fast for my 3-year-old to keep up with making new things. would be great if it stayed soft a little longer!

my experience was okay with this modeling clay. it molds well, but it does crumble easily and leaves a mess everywhere. it's fun for kids to play with, but just know you might be sweeping up a lot afterward. still, they love it!

this animal figurine set is super cute! i got the safari collection for my 5-year-old niece, and she loves playing with them during her pretend adventures. the only downside is that they are a bit smaller than i expected. still, the details are impressive!

saw these wildlife figurines at a friend's house and had to get some for my son. he plays with them daily. my only issue is that the paint chips off after a while. if your kid is gentle, they'll last longer!

this collection of animal action figures is adorable and lifelike! we have them all over the house, but some are a bit top-heavy and fall easily. overall, though, they’re perfect for my daughter’s imaginative play.

bought these collectible animal figures for my daughter’s birthday, and she was thrilled. however, after a week of play, a couple of them had started to lose fur. for the price, I expected better quality!

the details in these wildlife figurines are gorgeous! i bought the ocean set for my son, and he can’t get enough of them. just wish they had more options for different animals. still, it’s a solid purchase!
i love how realistic these action figures are! they sit on my shelf as part of my collection. they’re not toys for my kids, though, because they’re made of fragile materials. just be careful!

got these animal figurines for my classroom and the kids adore them! they use them for storytelling and role-playing. the only downside is they feel a bit lightweight and could break easily if dropped.
the plush animal figures are soft, but the arms and legs are a bit stiff, making it hard for my younger kids to pose them. they still enjoy playing with them, just wish they were more flexible!
who knew animal figurines would be such a hit? my kids love them! they’ve used them for school projects, too. the downside? a couple figures had paint smudges upon opening. definitely a mixed bag!
purchased the farm animal set for my toddler, and although they love playing with them, they tend to scatter everywhere! would appreciate some kind of storage bag to keep them organized.
these collectible animal figures have been a hit with my kids! they use them for pretend play and even in the bath! however, they’re not waterproof, which makes cleanup a bit of a hassle.
the quality of these action figures is impressive, but they were a little pricey for what they are. my son is obsessed with them, though! just wish they weren't so delicate.
just received the jungle animal figures. they’re adorable and the kids have played with them nonstop! the only issue is that the tails seem to be a bit flimsy. hope they don’t break off!
these plush animal figurines are cute, but I was expecting more detail. they’re not as vibrant as the pictures showed. good for snuggling, but not the best for playtime!

my daughter loves her new wildlife figurines! they’re perfect for her animal-themed birthday party. the only thing I didn’t expect was how many small parts some of them have. could be a choking hazard for younger kids.
just got the action figures for my nephew’s birthday. he loves them, but I wish they were a bit larger. I was worried about losing them, but he keeps them in his toy box, so it's fine!
the details on these animal figures are really nice, but some of them tip over easily. great for display, but not the best for rough play. they work better as decoration!
i bought the dinosaur figurines thinking my kids would love them, but they haven't played with them as much as I expected. they’re cute but a little too simple for their tastes.
these plush animal figures are great for younger kids, but the stitching started coming undone after only a month. my toddler loves them, but I want them to last longer!
got the safari collection and was impressed by the designs! however, my son accidentally ripped one while playing. they could use stronger stitching! still, they are good for imaginative play.
the animal figurines are very cute and well-made! my only complaint is that the prices for some are a bit steep. they’re nice, but I wouldn't have bought them at full price.
these action figures are not only fun but educational! my kids love learning about the animals while they play. wish they had more variety, though—would love to see more species!
just received the woodland creature set! they are absolutely adorable and the kids love them! the only downside is that they’re a little smaller than I pictured. still, a solid buy!
my kids loved these collectible figurines! they've been using them in their play rather than just as decoration. the downside is that a few figures came with slight paint defects. could be better!
the plush animal figures are super soft and perfect for cuddling! however, they’re not as durable as I hoped. my toddler has already managed to tear one’s ear! just need to supervise more.
I bought these animal figures for my wife as part of her decor. she loves them! the only issue? they are a little too delicate for her energetic cat. she’s got to keep them up high!
I got the farm animal action figures for my son, and he has loved them. they’re a bit small and the edges are sharp, so just keep an eye on little hands! perfect for imaginative fun, though.

just got the braun series 9 electric shaver and wow, it’s such an upgrade from my old one! it glides really smoothly and gives me a super close shave without irritation. i love that it has a cleaning base, but honestly, it’s a bit bulky for my bathroom counter. overall, though, it really makes my morning routine easier.

tried the Philips Norelco beard trimmer 7200 for a few months now. the vacuum feature is great at catching hair, but sometimes it clogs and needs to be cleared out. battery life is good, lasts about 60 minutes on a full charge, which is really convenient. just wish the attachments were a bit easier to snap on.

i was excited to use the bliss hair removal waxing kit, but it didn’t quite live up to my expectations. it heats up quickly, but the wax wasn't as effective on finer hair. i ended up having to go over the same area several times. on the plus side, it was nice to do this at home instead of scheduling a salon appointment.

received the epilady epilator as a gift and honestly, it takes some getting used to! it does pull a lot of hair out, which is great, but it hurts a bit more than I expected. i appreciate that it can work on sensitive areas, and the battery lasts a long time. just be prepared for some discomfort!

after using the Panasonic electric shaver for a month, i'm pretty happy with it. it gives a decent shave and is waterproof, which makes it easy to clean. however, it doesn’t have the closest shave compared to others I've tried. for me, it's a solid option for the price, about $50.

the Nair hair removal cream worked surprisingly well for me! I was worried about irritation because my skin is sensitive, but I had no issues at all. it took about 10 minutes and left my legs super smooth. my only issue is that the smell is pretty strong – definitely not pleasant – so ventilation is key!

my husband loves the Wahl beard trimmer! he finds it easy to use and appreciate the adjustable settings. however, it doesn’t trim as close as he’d like, especially in some areas. overall, it does the job but could use some improvements on the precision side.

i recently bought the Schick Hydro Silk Razor for women, and i absolutely love how it glides on my skin. it has a moisturizing strip that leaves my legs feeling soft. my only complaint is that it doesn’t last as long as i'd hoped; i find myself replacing it every month, which is a little annoying.

my experience with the Remington epilator was a mixed bag. it does remove hair well, but the pain is no joke! I think it works best for larger areas like legs, but for underarms, it’s a bit much for me. I appreciate the price point, though – it was under $40!

i have the Braun Silk expert pro 5, and man, it’s saved me so much time! it really works on my skin tone, but it takes a little while to see results. i love the design and the fact that i can use it at home. just keep in mind it’s a long-term investment for smooth skin.

the Veet sensitive hair removal cream was a total win for me! used it before my beach trip and my legs were smooth for days. it smells a bit strong, but it's worth it for the results. I do wish it came with a better applicator – it can get messy!

just tried the Philips Lumea IPL hair removal device! it’s so easy to use, but I’m only a few sessions in, so I can’t speak on long-term results yet. it does take some time to get through a full session. wish it had a bigger light window to cover larger areas faster!

the Tinkle eyebrow razors are a game changer! they’re small, easy to hold, and work so well for quick touch-ups. I keep them in my purse for emergencies. my only wish is that the blades didn’t dull so quickly; I feel like I need to replace them often.

got the Olay smooth finish facial hair removal duo and it works pretty good! the cream works well without irritation, but I find the applicator a bit awkward to use. it does its job for peach fuzz, but I wouldn’t rely on it for thicker hair. overall, not bad for the price!

the Braun Series 7 shaver has been a dream for my husband! the intelligence technology adjusts based on his facial hair density. the only downside is that it requires a bit of maintenance. for the price, about $200, it’s a great investment for those who care about grooming.

just opened the Norelco OneBlade trimmer, and it's super cool! it’s versatile for trimming and styling, but I do have to say that it doesn’t work as well on longer hair. I appreciate how easy it is to clean, though – pop the head off and rinse! worth a try if you want something different.

used the Sally Hansen wax strips for sensitive skin and they got the job done! they’re convenient for quick hair removal, but I wish they had a bit more wax on them. some hairs didn’t come off completely, so I had to go over it with a razor. but for the price, they’re pretty handy.

finally broke down and bought the Philips BRT382/15 bikini trimmer. it’s super cute and comes with multiple attachments! it does a good job of trimming down there, but I wouldn’t say it’s perfect for shaving. just keep in mind that it’s more of a trimmer than a shaver. still, it’s been nice for quick upkeep.

the Panasonics’ facial trimmer is fantastic for quick touch-ups. it’s gentle and easy to navigate around my eyebrows, but it’s definitely not for thick hair. I appreciate that it runs on a single battery, so I don’t have to worry about charging it. just make sure not to press too hard!

tried the Veet wax strips and I was surprised by how well they worked! pretty painless for the most part, but I still had to follow up with tweezers for a few stubborn hairs. they’re easy to use and the results lasted a few weeks, which is nice. perfect for a last-minute date night.

the Gillette Fusion ProGlide razor is pretty solid! I like the flexibility of the head, and it glides smoothly. my only issue is that the blades are a little pricey. I wish they would last longer, but for a close shave, I can’t complain too much. just keep an eye on your budget!

I received the Flawless Facial Hair Remover as a gift and it's surprisingly effective! it’s easy to handle, but I found it doesn’t work as well on thicker hair. I carry it in my bag for emergencies, and it’s great for a quick touch-up. just be careful not to go over the same spot too much.

the Braun Silk-épil 9 epilator is pretty good for the price. it’s efficient but man, it hurts sometimes! definitely for the brave. I love that it can be used wet or dry, which adds versatility. just wish it didn’t take so long to get through my legs.

my experience with the Philips beard trimmer has been mostly positive! it handles my beard well, but I found it a bit tricky to get the perfect length. battery life is solid! I can use it multiple times before recharging. just need a bit more practice to get my desired look.

Honestly, the Neutrogena Hydro Boost Gel Cream has been a lifesaver for my dry skin, especially during the winter months! It absorbs quickly and doesn't feel greasy, which is a huge plus for me. I’ve been using it for about 2 months now, and my skin feels so much softer. The only downside is the scent; it's a bit too artificial for my taste.

I tried the Paula's Choice Skin Perfecting 2% BHA Liquid Exfoliant, and while it did help with some of my blackheads, my skin felt a little irritated after a week of daily use. I love how lightweight it is and a little goes a long way, but I think I’ll stick to using it just a couple of times a week instead.

The La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk Sunscreen is great for keeping my skin protected without that heavy, greasy feel. It doesn't break me out, which is a miracle. However, I do wish it came in a bigger bottle for the price; I find myself going through it pretty quickly!

Have been using The Ordinary Hyaluronic Acid for about a month and it really has plumped up my skin! I love how affordable it is, too! The only con is that it can get a bit sticky if I apply too much. Just a little dab works wonders!

The Cetaphil Daily Hydrating Lotion is nice, but I didn’t find it quite moisturizing enough for my combo skin. It’s lightweight, which is good for summer, but by the end of the day, my cheeks feel tight. I think I’ll use this as a base under something thicker during winter.

I recently got the Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Cream, and wow, my skin feels so soft and smooth. It's only been a couple of weeks, but I can already see some fine lines fading. On the downside, the fragrance is a little strong, which is off-putting at times.

Bioré UV Aqua Rich Watery Essence is hands down the best sunscreen I've ever used! It feels like a moisturizer rather than a sunscreen and doesn’t leave a white cast. My only issue is that it can get a bit oily after a few hours; I need to set it with powder.

The Tatcha The Dewy Skin Cream is like a dream for dry skin! It’s rich and hydrating without being too heavy. I use it at night and wake up with glowing skin. The only downside is the price—definitely a splurge, but I feel like it’s worth it.

I gave CeraVe Hydrating Cleanser a try after hearing so many good things, and it’s a solid choice! It’s gentle and doesn’t strip my skin at all, but I don’t love the texture; it feels a bit too thick for my taste. Still, it does its job well.

The Drunk Elephant Protini Polypeptide Cream is fantastic; my skin feels firmer and more hydrated! However, it can be a bit harsh on my sensitive spots, especially when I mix it with retinol. I’m still figuring out the right balance, but overall, I’m pleased.

The Supergoop! Unseen Sunscreen is a game changer! It’s so lightweight, and it doubles as a primer under makeup. I just wish it came in a bigger size; I feel like I’m going to run out quickly and it's not the cheapest option.

First off, I really like the texture of First Aid Beauty's Hello FAB Coconut Skin Smoothie Priming Moisturizer. It gives my skin a nice glow! But I have to say, it’s not very long-lasting for my makeup. By noon, my face looks a bit oily, which is disappointing.

I’ve been trying out the L'Oréal Paris Revitalift 10% Vitamin C Serum, and I’m impressed! The brightness it gives my skin is amazing, but it can feel a bit sticky if I layer too much. Just one pump is all I need!

The Bliss Lemon & Sage Body Butter smells heavenly and really hydrates my skin! Perfect for my dry elbows and knees. However, I wish it soaked in faster because I don’t like waiting around before getting dressed.

The SkinCeuticals C E Ferulic Serum is pricey but truly delivers on the brightness! I noticed a difference in my dark spots within just a month. However, I have to be careful with how much I use; too much can feel heavy and can slightly irritate my sensitive skin.

The Aveeno Positively Radiant Daily Moisturizer with SPF 30 is solid for everyday use! It provides decent hydration and protection, but sometimes it leaves a slight white cast. It works best for me if I apply a tinted moisturizer on top.

I like the feel of the Olay Total Effects Whip Moisturizer. It's light and fluffy, and feels nice on my skin. That said, I didn’t see much difference in my skin's appearance after a few weeks, which was a bit disappointing.

The Mary Kay TimeWise Age-Fighting Moisturizer has a great texture and feels luxurious on the skin! I’ve noticed my skin is smoother, but then again, it is a bit pricey for my budget. Still kind of worth it if you’re looking for an upgrade.

After using the Paula's Choice 10% Niacinamide Booster, I've seen a noticeable reduction in pore size, which is exciting! However, it can sometimes cause a mild tingling sensation, so I have to be careful and test it on a small area first.

Love the consistency of this Tula Skincare 24-7 Moisture Hydrating Day & Night Cream! It feels so nourishing. My skin drinks it up immediately. My only complaint is that it doesn’t have SPF, so I still need to layer sunscreen on top.

The Eucerin Advanced Repair Lotion has been great for my super dry skin during the winter! It’s thick and rich without feeling greasy. However, the pump can be a bit tricky; it doesn’t always dispense properly, which can be annoying.

I’ve recently started using the Mario Badescu Facial Spray with Aloe, Herbs, and Rosewater, and I love the refreshing feel! It’s great for a mid-day pick-me-up. But I do wish it had a finer mist; sometimes it feels a bit splashy.

The Herbivore Botanicals Prism 20% AHA + 5% BHA Exfoliating Glow Serum is a standout! My skin looks bright and feels so soft the next day. But be careful if you have sensitive skin; I experienced some redness after the first use. Just patch test it first!

I got the Murad Hydro-Dynamic Ultimate Moisture Cream on a whim, and I’m really glad I did! It’s super hydrating and lasts all day. My only complaint is that it doesn’t come with a spatula, and I prefer not to dip my fingers in the jar.

The Kiehl's Ultra Facial Cream is a solid moisturizer, especially for my dry areas. It’s not too heavy, which is nice. However, it doesn’t do much for oily areas; I still find my forehead shiny by afternoon, which is disappointing. Overall, it’s just okay for my skin type.

this foaming cleanser is a game changer for my morning routine! it really wakes me up and leaves my skin feeling fresh. however, i wish it had a bit more moisturizing properties, especially in winter.

i've been using this oil cleanser for a week now, and i'm loving it! it melts away my makeup effortlessly. just make sure to follow up with a gentle toner because it can leave my skin feeling a tad greasy.
this gel cleanser is okay. cleans well but doesn’t seem to help with my breakouts like i hoped. it’s super gentle though, so i appreciate that!
picked up this facial cleanser after my friend raved about it. it's decent, my skin feels clean but not overly stripped. the scent is refreshing! just wish it was a bit more hydrating.
been using this cleanser for about 2 months now. my skin feels smooth, and it's great for oily skin. i do have to moisturize afterwards because it can feel tight.
this is my go-to foaming cleanser! it really helps keep my skin clear. i just wish the bottle lasted longer, i feel like i run out too quickly!
total game changer for my nightly routine! the oil cleanser takes everything off. just a heads up, it can be messy to use if you're not careful.
the gel cleanser feels nice but i'm not sure it's reducing my acne like i wanted. it’s smooth and doesn’t irritate my skin, but i need more action.
love the price of this foaming cleanser. it’s super affordable and feels luxurious! but, sometimes it can be a bit too bubbly, and i feel like i have to rinse forever.
the oil cleanser is good, but i feel like i have to use a lot more than i thought. it's effective but sometimes makes my skin feel a bit heavy afterwards.
got this gel cleanser for sensitive skin and it's been great! my skin feels clean without irritation. just a bit on the pricey side for the size.
this foaming cleanser really helped my skin, but it can be a little drying, especially in winter. wish it had a bit more hydration!
the facial cleanser has a nice lather and smells amazing! however, it hasn't done much for my blackheads, which is a bummer.
i’ve tried a ton of cleansers and this one is solid. doesn't break me out and feels gentle. however, the pump could use some work, it gets stuck sometimes.
this oil cleanser is super effective for removing makeup. however, it left my skin feeling a bit oily after rinsing. i guess that's the trade-off!
this cleanser has been in my routine for almost a year! my skin looks so much healthier. just wish it was available in a bigger bottle.
the gel cleanser is refreshing, but it doesn’t lather much, which surprised me. i’m used to that foam! it feels nice but still trying to decide if i love it.
this foaming cleanser smells heavenly! i love how it feels on my skin, but sometimes it leaves my face feeling a bit tight.
just started using this oil cleanser; it feels unique compared to my usual ones. while it removes makeup well, it can be tricky to rinse off completely.
this gel cleanser promised a lot, but it didn’t deliver on reducing my pores like i hoped. still decent for daily use though!
i love the packaging of this facial cleanser, it looks so chic in my bathroom. the cleanser itself is just okay, a little drying after use.
the foaming cleanser is really nice, but the scent is a bit strong for my liking. effective but just too perfumy for me.
this oil cleanser is definitely a treat! it glides smoothly and feels luxurious. however, it can leave a film if you don't rinse thoroughly.
have been using the gel cleanser for a few weeks. works great, but i noticed a slight burning sensation initially. make sure to patch test, i guess!
picked up this foaming cleanser because of the reviews. it’s great but don’t expect miracles on stubborn makeup. it’s more of a daily cleanser for me.
the facial cleanser does a decent job, but the pump is so annoying. it takes a lot of effort to get product out! would be perfect with a different design.
this oil cleanser is my lifesaver after a long day! it takes off all the makeup with ease. just make sure to follow up with a good wash to avoid any residue.
the gel cleanser is refreshing but not effective for my oily skin. might need to switch to something heavier duty. overall, nice for a gentle cleanse.
this foaming cleanser is luxurious and makes my skin feel fresh. but it’s a bit pricey, so I’m not sure I'll repurchase once I run out.
tried this facial cleanser on a whim, and I actually like it! it's gentle and doesn’t break me out, but I wish it was more effective on my blackheads.

the CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser has been a lifesaver for my dry skin. it feels gentle and doesn’t strip my face like other cleansers. I’ve been using it for about 2 months now, and my skin feels so much softer. but the pump can be a bit finicky sometimes, so I have to be careful when I use it.

I've tried a lot of moisturizers, but Neutrogena Hydro Boost is different. I love how light it feels on my skin, and it absorbs super quickly. I’ve been using it for about 3 weeks, and I can see a difference in my hydration. however, I wish it offered a bit more SPF protection since I'm out in the sun a lot.

the Thayers Witch Hazel Toner is a staple in my skincare routine. it really refreshes my skin and helps control oil throughout the day. I've noticed my pores look a little smaller since I started using it a month ago. the only downside is that I wish it came in a larger bottle, as I go through it pretty fast.

got the La Roche-Posay Pure Vitamin C Face Serum to treat some dark spots, and I can say it works pretty well! I’ve noticed a lightening of them over 6 weeks. the texture is nice, and it layers well under makeup. though, the scent is a bit strong for my taste, but overall, I’d say it's worth trying.

the Mario Badescu Facial Spray is something I didn't think I needed but now can’t live without! I use it right after cleansing and throughout the day for a quick refresh. I love the rose scent! that said, it doesn’t do much for hydration, so I still need a moisturizer after.

I recently started using the Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Cream, and my skin feels noticeably firmer. it’s creamy without being greasy, which is a win for me! I’ve only been using it for about 2 weeks, but my fine lines seem to be less visible. my only complaint is the jar packaging; I prefer pump bottles to keep it sanitary.

the ArtNaturals Organic Facial Cleanser has a lovely natural scent and feels super refreshing. I love that it’s free from harsh chemicals, but sometimes it leaves my skin feeling a little tight afterward. I think it’s better suited for oily skin; mine’s combination, so I'm still on the fence.

after hearing all the hype, I tried The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%. I like how it helps control my oil production and makes my skin look smoother. I’ve been using it for about a month, but it can be irritating if I use too much, so I have to be careful.

the Green Tea Moisturizer from Innisfree is one of my favorites! it’s lightweight and feels nice on my skin without feeling heavy. I’ve been using it daily for 3 months and my skin looks more glowy! however, it does have a bit of a strong scent, so be aware if you’re sensitive to fragrances.

tried the Tatcha The Water Cream and oh wow! it feels like silk on my skin. I love how it hydrates without making me oily. I’ve used it for a couple of weeks now and my makeup sits beautifully on top. the price is quite steep though, so I’ll have to save for my next jar.

I bought the Paula’s Choice Skin Perfecting 2% BHA Liquid Exfoliant and was nervous at first. it helped with my acne and smooth texture, but I had to start slow with it or my skin would get red and irritated. after 3 weeks, I think I’ve gotten the hang of it! wish I hadn’t had to go through that learning curve.

the Burt's Bees Intense Hydration Night Cream is amazing for nighttime. I wake up with my skin feeling so soft! but I don’t love the scent, it’s a bit too earthy for my taste. overall, it does the job well though and I’ve been using it for about a month with great results.

the Neutrogena Pore Refining Toner is a decent product, but it hasn’t been a miracle worker for my pores. I’ve been using it for 3 weeks and while it feels refreshing, I don’t see drastic changes. it helps with oiliness though, so I still might keep it around.

I really wanted to like the Simple Micellar Cleansing Water but it left a greasy residue on my skin. I have sensitive skin, so it didn't irritate me, but I expected it to cleanse better. it comes in a large bottle and lasts quite a while, so I might use it on days when I wear less makeup.

the Fresh Rose Deep Hydration Face Cream has a luxurious feel to it. I love using it after my serum in the morning! it’s hydrating without being too thick. I’ve been using it for 1 month and my skin looks brighter. but the price is a bit high for me, not sure if I’d repurchase.

I’ve been using the Garnier SkinActive Micellar Cleansing Water for a while now, and it does a great job of removing makeup! it’s gentle and doesn’t sting my eyes, which is a plus. however, I found it doesn’t always get rid of waterproof mascara completely, so I have to use something else for that.

the Klairs Supple Preparation Facial Toner is super soothing! I’ve noticed a significant difference in my skin’s hydration levels since I started using it. been about 2 months now, but I do wish it came in a larger size since I feel like I’m refilling it too often.

I gave the Tula Skincare Hydrating Day & Night Cream a try and loved how it feels! my skin is feeling plump after just a few uses. the only downside is the price; it’s pretty pricey for the amount you get, so I’ll have to save it for special occasions.

I recently started using the Skinceuticals C E Ferulic serum, and I can see why people rave about it! my skin looks brighter and feels firmer after just a few weeks. the only negative is the cost; it’s quite steep, so I hope it lasts long enough to justify the price.

the First Aid Beauty Pure Skin Face Cleanser is a gentle yet effective option for my sensitive skin. I love how it cleans without stripping moisture. I’ve been using it for about a month, but I wish it came in a more convenient packaging instead of a squeeze tube.

I heard great things about the Kiehl’s Ultra Facial Cream and I was excited to try it out. it’s super moisturizing and feels great, but I also noticed it can leave a bit of a shiny finish. after a few weeks, I think it’s best for nighttime use rather than under makeup.

the L’Oreal Revitalift Serum has been a staple for a couple of months now. love the feel and it absorbs nicely without leaving a tacky finish. I’m noticing a bit of firmness, but at times, it can feel a bit heavy if I apply too much, so I’m learning how much to use.

the Algenist Genius Liquid Collagen is really interesting! it feels luxurious and absorbs so well, making my skin feel plump. however, the price is pretty steep and I’m not entirely sold on whether it’s worth it just yet since I haven’t seen drastic changes after 3 weeks.

these organic cotton pads are a lifesaver! the softness is amazing, and they don’t irritate my sensitive skin at all. i usually go through a box of 14 in about 2 months. the only downside is they take longer to absorb on really heavy days, so I still keep some regular ones on hand for when things get intense.

i wanted to love these menstrual cups after hearing all the hype, but honestly, it took me a while to find the right fit. after a couple of months, i've gotten the hang of it, but insertion can still be tricky for me sometimes. the eco-friendly angle is great though, considering how many tampons i used to go through!

these sanitary pads are pretty decent! they’re super absorbent and have a great adhesive, but they’re a bit bulkier than I expected. i can feel them through my leggings, which isn’t ideal. if you’re looking for comfort and not too worried about discretion, then they’re a solid choice.

not a fan of these tampons. they leak more than i’d like, especially on my second day, which is super frustrating. i love the applicator though; it’s very smooth and makes insertion easy. but if they don't keep up with my flow, what’s the point?

finally found the perfect menstrual cup! it took a few tries to get it inserted right, but now it lasts me all day without leaks. i love that i can go about my day without worrying. just wish there were more color options; clear is so boring!

these pads are life-changing during nighttime! they’re long and super absorbent, plus they stay in place all night. the only thing is they can be a bit crinkly, which makes noise when I move. not a huge deal if I’m sleeping alone, but still kind of annoying.

i've tried a ton of different menstrual cups and this one is by far the most comfortable. it has a flexible rim that makes it easy to insert and remove. however, it can be a pain to clean properly. i feel like i'm always worried about it not being totally sanitized.

tampons are usually my go-to, but i wanted to try these organic ones for a change. they do feel softer and more comfortable, but they tend to expand a bit too much for my liking. sometimes they feel like they’re too full too quickly, which is uncomfortable.

the high absorbency of these pads is impressive! they work great during my heaviest days. they’re also pretty comfortable, but I do wish they were a little thinner. it would be nice to feel a bit more discreet, especially under tighter clothes.

just started using this menstrual cup and I’m amazed! it took a couple of cycles to figure it out but now I can go 12 hours without a worry. the only downside is the cleanup process can be a little messy, but it's worth it for the eco benefits!

these sanitary pads feel very soft and breathable, which is a plus. however, I find they sometimes shift around a bit during the day, so I have to adjust them. definitely not the worst, but not my absolute favorite either.

i have mixed feelings about these tampons. they absorb well, but I think I might be allergic to something in them because I’ve noticed irritation. will probably switch back to my regular brand, which is a bummer because I wanted to try something new!

finally gave these organic cotton pads a try and i'm so happy! they don’t have that weird chemical smell, and they feel gentle on my skin. the only bummer is that they don’t seem as absorbent as my regular ones, so I have to change them more often.

found these pads a bit too thick for my taste. they absorb well and keep me dry, but I feel like I’m wearing a diaper sometimes. if you need heavy protection, these might be your best bet, just not the most comfortable for me.

i was skeptical about menstrual cups but this one changed my mind! it took some practice, but now I can wear it for up to 12 hours without leaks. however, I do wish there were better instructions; the packaging was a bit vague on how to care for it.

these washable pads are great for the environment, but I’m still getting used to the whole cleaning process. they hold up well, but there’s something about washing blood that makes me cringe. overall, I think it’s a good option for those who can handle it!

the convenience of these tampons is unmatched! love that I can just toss them in my bag and go. but sometimes they feel a bit rough going in compared to others I’ve tried. not unbearable, but definitely noticeable.

i enjoy these menstrual cups; they feel much cleaner than tampons. however, they’re a little hard to remove, and sometimes I get a little anxious about whether or not I’ve positioned it correctly. it’s all about figuring out what works best for you!

these pads have been my go-to for years now. they’re comfortable and the absorbency is generally solid. my only issue is they seem to slide around if I’m too active, which can be a hassle. I’m still searching for that perfect pad!

i really wanted to love this menstrual cup, but it’s been a struggle. it feels bulky and I find it difficult to get a proper seal, leading to leaks. I might have to try a different brand to find the right fit for my body.

just picked up these organic cotton pads and wow, they are super soft! I love that they’re eco-friendly. on the downside, they don’t seem to hold as much as the regular ones, so I have to change more often during my heaviest days.

tried these tampons on a whim and I’m really impressed! they expand nicely and I didn’t experience any leaks at all. my only con is that I wish they had a more sleek applicator; it's a bit bulky. but overall a great buy!

I used these pads for my whole cycle and they worked pretty well. I love that they’re breathable, but they could be a little more absorbent. I found myself needing to change during the day more often than I like.

had high hopes for this menstrual cup, but it seems to take me way too long to get it to seal properly. when it does work, it’s awesome, but the hassle of potential leaks means I often go back to tampons. maybe I'll try again later.

these pads are pretty decent, and I appreciate the bulk pricing. they hold up during the day for the most part, but they are a bit noisy, which can be awkward when you’re in a quiet setting. good for the money though.

i switched to these menstrual cups for the eco-friendly factor and they’re a game changer for sure! but the learning curve is real; it took me a couple of cycles to feel confident in using it, but now it's smooth sailing. a bit messy at times, but I just love the freedom it gives me!

this colgate total toothpaste really gives me that fresh feeling! i’ve been using it for about 2 months now, and my mouth feels cleaner throughout the day. the only thing is, the flavor is a bit strong for me; sometimes it burns my tongue a little. overall, a solid toothpaste, just wish it wasn’t so intense!

i'm a big fan of this listerine mouthwash! it packs a punch and makes me feel like my mouth is ultra clean. however, the burn can be a bit much, especially right after brushing. but it really seems to help with my gum health, so i’ll keep using it!

the oral-b pro 1000 electric toothbrush has been a lifesaver. my dentist noticed a difference in my gum health after just 3 months! the only downside is that it takes a while to charge and can be a bit bulky to carry while traveling.

i was excited to try these reach mint waxed dental flossers. they get in between my teeth really well, but they break easily, which is super frustrating! they’re convenient and great for on-the-go, just wish the floss was sturdier.

just finished my first tube of tom's of maine natural toothpaste and honestly, i’m a bit torn. it does feel great knowing it’s natural, but i miss the strong minty taste of regular toothpaste. it cleans well, but i find myself reaching for my old brand sometimes.

this sensodyne toothpaste for sensitive teeth has made a noticeable difference in my daily routine! after a week of use, my sensitivity has greatly reduced. still, the flavor is somewhat bland and doesn’t leave that fresh aftertaste i’m used to.

my kids love the bubble gum flavor of this hello kids toothpaste! it’s made brushing fun for them, but as a parent, i wish it had more fluoride. it’s a great option for making dental care enjoyable, just needs that extra protective boost.

the waterpik cordless plus water flosser is a fantastic addition to my oral care regimen! i noticed a difference in plaque control within weeks. my only complaint would be that the battery life isn’t as long as i’d hoped; i find myself charging it every few days.

the crest 3d white strips are amazing! after just 2 weeks, my teeth are noticeably whiter. but the sensitivity they cause can be a real bummer. i usually stick to using them every other day to manage that.

i recently switched to the quip toothbrush and it’s a game changer for travel. it’s slim and easy to pack! just wish the vibrating timer was a little louder; sometimes i don’t hear it over the water running.

the charmin dental floss is decent, but it glides a bit too easily, causing it to snap occasionally. it does get the job done, but i find myself using twice as much as with other brands. a good option, just not my favorite.

love this native mouthwash, especially the natural ingredients! it feels good knowing there aren't any harsh chemicals. the only downside is that the flavor can be a bit weak compared to traditional mouthwashes, so I usually find myself rinsing longer.

i’ve tried a lot of toothpaste over the years, but this baking soda one from arm & hammer is unique! it does a good job at cleaning and whitening, however, the gritty texture took some getting used to. feels like a mini scrub for my teeth!
the reach toothbrush with tongue cleaner is pretty nifty. my breath feels fresher after brushing! i do wish the bristles were softer, though; sometimes they feel a bit harsh on my gums.

these floss picks from gum are super handy for travel! they’re easy to use and fit perfectly in my purse. however, the plastic feels a little flimsy and sometimes they break when I’m trying to use them. still a good backup option!

my dentist recommended this biotene mouthwash for dryness issues, and wow, what a difference! it’s been a month, and I feel so much better. only downside is the flavor isn’t my favorite...but I’ll take relief over taste any day.

the act kids' mouthwash is a hit at my house! my little ones actually look forward to using it. the only issue is I feel it runs out pretty quickly—about a week and it’s gone with my 4-year-old’s enthusiastic use!
the philips sonicare toothbrush is worth the hype. after just a month, I noticed less plaque buildup. my only issue is the price! it's a bit steep, but if it keeps my teeth healthy, I'm okay with it.

the tom's of maine crystal toothpaste was a new experience for me! i like that it’s natural, but the taste was a little odd and not as refreshing as my usual brands. i’ll probably stick to it for now but may explore other options later.

this oral-b glide floss is great at getting between tight spaces. the mint flavor is a nice touch too. however, it can get a bit tangled up, which is frustrating. good for cleaning but wish it were more durable!

my 6-year-old loves the fun flavors of this kids’ toothpaste! however, I wish it had a bit more fluoride. it’s a good option for kids, but i’m constantly checking the label to make sure it’s doing its job.
the sonicare airfloss is such a cool gadget! I love how it makes flossing faster. the only downside is it took some time to figure out how to use it properly, but now that I have, it’s great!

this mymagic toothpaste made my teeth feel super clean! i noticed a brightness after a few uses. that said, the flavor isn’t my favorite and feels slightly medicinal compared to what I’m used to, but the results are worth it!

the dr. bronner's toothpaste is a unique find! I love the organic ingredients, but the texture is different than what I expected—more like a gel. it took some adjustment, but overall it leaves my mouth feeling fresh.
this gum detoxify toothpaste really works wonders! my gums feel healthier after just a few weeks. i do wish it foamed a bit more while brushing, but the results keep me coming back for more.
my husband loves the mint flavor of this colgate toothpaste, but I find it too strong sometimes. it cleans well, no doubt about that! just wish it had a gentler aftertaste for my sensitive palette.

the oral-b interspace toothbrush is great for reaching tight spots! i feel like I’m cleaning well, but the head is a bit bigger than I’d like. takes a bit more effort to maneuver, but it does the job!

this natural toothpaste from hello is a fun addition to my routine! the flavor is delightful. however, I feel like it doesn’t offer as much stain removal as some other brands, but I enjoy the clean ingredients.

my son got this fire station playset for his 4th birthday, and he absolutely loves it! the little fire truck is adorable and has a ladder that can extend, which he thinks is super cool. the only downside is that the doors can be a bit stiff for him to open. otherwise, it's sturdy and has held up well against his rough play.

i ordered this pirate ship playhouse for my daughter’s birthday, and it was a huge hit! she loves that it has multiple levels and fun accessories. one thing though, the sails are pretty flimsy and don’t seem to hold up with rough play. but overall, it’s been a great addition to her toy collection!

my kids got this dinosaur park playset, and honestly, it’s a mixed bag. the details are amazing and they love the little dinosaurs, but setting up the track is really complicated. it took us about an hour just to get everything together, and we lost a few pieces in the process. they still play with it, just wish it wasn’t so tedious to set up.

i recently purchased the castle tower playhouse for my 5-year-old, and he loves pretending to be a knight. the designs are colorful, and there's a dragon that adds extra fun! my only complaint is that the flags tend to fall off easily, which drives him a little nuts, but overall, it’s been a great toy for creative play.

this space station set is pretty cool! my 6-year-old loves the rocket ship and all the little astronaut figures. it’s a great way to encourage imaginative play. however, the snap-together pieces don’t fit as securely as I’d like, so we have to handle it gently. still, they have tons of fun with it!

we got this animal rescue playset, and while my 3-year-old likes it, I find it a bit basic. the animals are cute, but there aren’t enough accessories to make the setup exciting. it's good for simple play, but I wish there was more variety to keep him engaged longer.

purchased the construction vehicles playset for my toddler, and he’s in love! the trucks are chunky enough for his little hands to manage, and the sounds they make are fun. the only thing I don’t like is that the batteries died really quickly – we’ve only had it a month. overall, still a solid choice for young builders!

we bought the fairy garden playhouse for my daughter, and she adores it! the little fairy figures are adorable, but the setup was a bit tricky, and some pieces did feel a little flimsy at first. it’s held up so far, but we’ll see how it goes after a few more weeks of play.

for my son's birthday, I got him this dinosaur-themed playset, and he’s been playing with it non-stop for weeks! I love the variety of dinosaurs included, but some of the accessories seem a bit cheap, like they might break easily. still, it's been a great toy overall, just wish they’d used sturdier materials.

i bought this jungle explorer playset for my grandson, and he was thrilled! the helicopter is super fun, and the action figures are well made. only downside is it can be a bit challenging to put everything together, especially the jungle scenery. once it's set up, though, they have a blast!

my kids received the fairy tale castle set, and they love it! it’s bright and colorful, which is a plus. however, the roof pieces don’t stay on very well, which can be frustrating during playtime. but they have fun creating their own stories, so I can overlook the roof issue.

the superhero city playhouse was a gift for my 8-year-old, and he thinks it's awesome! however, some of the smaller pieces are a bit hard to find once they fall. the city layout is cool, just wish it included a few more vehicles. but he plays with it almost daily, so I can't complain too much.

got this farm playset for my 4-year-old, and it's fun! the animals are cute, and he loves playing farm. one issue is that the barn doesn't close properly, and sometimes it gets frustrating. overall, it serves its purpose well, just has a few quirks we didn't expect.

my daughter loves the princess castle playhouse, and it's super cute! but I have to say, the plastic isn’t as high quality as I hoped, and some of the pieces don’t fit together as nicely as they should. still, she spends hours playing, so I guess it’s a win!

the train station playset is a hit with my son! he loves the moving train, and it’s made his afternoon playtime more exciting. however, some of the tracks seem to pop apart too easily, which can interrupt play. still, he enjoys it enough to keep coming back for more.

this underwater adventure playset was a gift for my niece, and she’s excited about it! the marine creatures are well-designed, but I wish the scenery parts were a bit sturdier; they feel a little flimsy on the edges. she makes up great stories with it, though, so it gets her imagination going!

i picked up this volcano playset for my son, and it's been fun! the erupting feature is a big hit. however, the setup took forever, and one of the lava pieces split after just a week of play. he still enjoys it, but I just wish it was a bit more durable.

my kids love the sports arena playset! the figures are really detailed, and they spent hours pretending to compete. my main issue is that some parts are small and can be easily lost, which is frustrating. it would be nice if they included a storage compartment or something.

the police station playset was such a great gift for my 6-year-old! he loves the working lights and sounds. one downside is that the doors can get jammed sometimes, but he doesn’t mind too much. it’s been a good toy for him to role-play with friends.

after getting this jungle playhouse for my 5-year-old, I can say it’s a mix of great fun and irritation. she loves the animals and the treehouse, but the pieces don’t stick together well, which makes it annoying during play. overall, she's enjoying it, just wish it was a bit more robust.

our kid received the beach hut playset, and it’s adorable! the details are vibrant, and the surfboards are a fun touch. however, the sandcastle piece broke after a few days, which was disappointing. they still play with it daily, but I would've liked better durability.

we got this construction site playset for my son, and he’s been obsessed! the trucks are big and sturdy, but the building pieces are not as tough as I expected. some broke within the first week, which was a bummer. he still loves it, just wish they’d made it tougher.

this space exploration set is so much fun for my 7-year-old! he loves the rocket launch and the alien figures. however, the assembly was a headache, and I wish the rocket had a bit more storage space for the pieces. regardless, he plays with it every day, so it's a win overall.

my 5-year-old absolutely loves this dress up set! it comes with a chef hat, apron, and tons of accessories for her toy kitchen. we’ve spent countless hours roleplaying different meals. the only downside is that the fabric feels a bit thin, so i wonder how long it'll last with all her cooking adventures.

we got this for our almost 4-year-old, and while she enjoys the doctor set, the stethoscope broke within a week. she still loves pretending to be a doctor though, and the other accessories are sturdy. just wish the stethoscope was better quality since it's such an important part of the set.

the pirate costume was a huge hit at my son's dress up party! the material was much better than I expected for about $25. he loved the hat and sword, but I did notice the belt was a little small, which made it difficult for him to wear comfortably. overall, it sparked so much creative storytelling!

this kitchen playset is so cute and perfect for my 3-year-old! it has bright colors and lots of different food items. however, the oven door doesn’t close properly, which is a bit disappointing. still, she has a blast pretending to cook and serve meals to her stuffed animals.

my daughter loves pretending to be a teacher with this set! the mini chalkboard and all the student supplies are adorable. one thing that could be improved is the quality of the tabletop; it scratches easily. but, for the price, it gets a lot of use in our house!

got this dressing up box for my twins and they’ve really enjoyed it! the variety of outfits encourages them to role-play together. the only issue is that some of the sizes don’t fit quite right; one of my kids is 4 and the other is 6, and the clothes can be a little tight on the bigger one.

we’ve had this toy kitchen for about 6 months now, and it has held up pretty well despite my 2-year-old’s rough play. i love that it has so many accessories. however, some of the food items are already starting to show wear and tear. overall, it’s still a solid buy for imaginative play.

the fairy costume is super cute and my daughter feels like a princess in it! the wings were a bit flimsy though; they bent easily. she still loves twirling around in it, but I wish it came with a stronger set of wings to match the outfit's charm.

I got this dress-up trunk for my little one’s birthday, and wow, it’s a treasure trove! from pirate hats to superhero capes, there's so much fun packed in. the only problem is that some of the items are a bit small, so they might not last as my child grows older.

my son loves his astronaut costume! it comes with a helmet that fits well and a jumpsuit that’s fun to wear. but, after just a few wears, the stitching around the cuffs started coming loose, which is disappointing for the price. overall, it’s still a fun outfit for imaginative space adventures.

this fireman costume is adorable, and my 4-year-old feels so brave wearing it! the hat has a cool design, but it’s a little on the small side and doesn’t fit his big head very well. love the fire extinguisher toy that came with it, though – super cute!

the magic set included in the dress-up box has been a blast! my daughter loves putting on magic shows for the family. however, some of the tricks are a little too complex for her age (5), which led to some frustration. still, it’s a great way to encourage creativity.

this dentist dress-up kit is a great tool for role-playing! my son loves pretending to clean teeth. the only complaint I have is that the toothbrush started falling apart after a couple of uses. other than that, it sparks plenty of imaginative fun!

the police officer costume was an instant favorite for my 6-year-old! the badge is shiny, which he loves. but, the walkie-talkie stopped working after a few hours of playtime, which was a bummer. still, he runs around the house pretending to catch bad guys all day!

we purchased this pirate dress-up kit, and it’s such a fun addition! the fabric of the costume feels nice, and the sword included has held up well. however, the eye patch didn’t fit very well and kept slipping off. overall, it encourages such fun adventures!

the cooking set is a hit in our house! my 3-year-old loves all the mini food items and utensils. the only downside is that some pieces are a little small; I worry about her choking on them. still, it inspires amazing creativity during playtime!

this princess dress is beautiful, but I wish the quality was better. after just a few wears, the seams started to come undone. my daughter is mixed on it because she loves how it looks, but the quality might not hold up for long. it’s a shame because it’s such a pretty outfit.

the superhero cape and mask set is great! my son wears it everywhere and has so much fun saving the day. the only issue is that the mask doesn't fit very well, so I’ve had to adjust it a lot. still, it’s such a fun way to boost his imagination.

I bought this doctor’s kit for my 4-year-old, and it’s been a total winner! the stethoscope and thermometer feel real, which adds to the play experience. however, the plastic on some tools feels a bit cheap. but hey, it has encouraged so much role-playing!

the play kitchen we got is really cute and has tons of accessories! however, it took me a while to put it together, and I wish the instructions were clearer. my kids still love it though and play with it every day, so that makes it worth it!

I was excited about this dancer costume, but it runs a bit small. My daughter is 5, and it barely fits her. She loves it, but I worry it won’t last long. The color and design are gorgeous, though, so part of me still thinks it’s worth keeping.

my kids really enjoyed this camping set! it encouraged them to build their own pretend campfire. only downside? the tents are a bit flimsy and kept falling over. still, they’ve had a blast pretending to roast marshmallows and tell stories, so I can't complain too much!

the magical unicorn costume is a big win for my daughter! she loves the sparkly fabric and the tail. the only thing is that the headband is a little tight. despite that, she prances around imagining all sorts of magical adventures!

this toy kitchen has brought so much playtime joy! my 2-year-old loves cooking alongside us and imitating what we do. however, the knobs on the stove are a bit hard for her to turn. it’s still a great tool for sparking her creativity when we play together.

the firefighter costume is quite popular with my son! I appreciate the reflective stripes for added realism. however, the helmet feels a bit flimsy and doesn't stay on his head very well. overall, it’s still great for imaginative fire-fighting plays!

I bought this vet set for my daughter, and it’s adorable! she loves caring for her stuffed animals. but, the stethoscope doesn’t seem to work as well as advertised. still, it’s a fun way to encourage nurturing and role-playing, so I’m happy with it.

this dollhouse is a total dream! the scale models are cute and the miniature furniture is well-detailed. my daughter loves rearranging everything. it took us about an hour to set up, and the instructions were pretty clear. just wish the roof stayed on better; it tends to slide off easily when we play.

we purchased this dollhouse for our 4-year-old, and while she enjoys it, I found some of the miniature furniture a bit flimsy. one of the chairs broke after a week of play. overall, it's charming and colorful but I wish the materials were more durable for kids.

love the architectural design of this dollhouse! it's so stylish and looks great in my daughter's room. the scale models of the furniture are adorable, but the doors don't always shut properly, which can be frustrating during playtime. still worth it for the aesthetics!

my son loves playing with this dollhouse. it has a great layout and the interior decoration is vibrant. however, the stickers for the wallpaper peeled off after a few weeks. really wish they were better quality because the house looks so much better with them!

this dollhouse exceeded my expectations! the tiny kitchen accessories are just perfect. my daughter spends hours playing with them. one downside is the assembly took forever. I consider myself handy, but it took about two hours. overall, it’s a beautifully designed set.

the dollhouse is cute, but my 3-year-old found some of the pieces challenging to manipulate. the furniture is small, and a couple of items got lost pretty quickly. I love the creative aspect, but I wish there were larger options for younger kids.

okay, so this dollhouse is super adorable! the furniture looks like it came from a fancy store. my daughter loves decorating the rooms. the only thing that bugs me is that the floors aren't very sturdy; they creak a bit when you move around.

we got this dollhouse after reading a lot of positive reviews, and I wanted to love it, but the wood splintered a bit during assembly. it has great style, but I’m concerned about durability as my kids tend to be a bit rough. also, some furniture was missing!

the level of detail in this dollhouse is impressive! my kids loved the miniature furniture that fits perfectly in each room. however, after playing for a month, some pieces started to come apart. I guess that’s expected with heavy play, but it’s a bummer.

I bought this for my niece's birthday and she was over the moon! the architectural design is so chic and the furniture is adorable. downside? the paint on a few pieces chipped right out of the box, which was disappointing. overall, cute but be careful.

this dollhouse has brought so much joy to my kids. they spend hours playing with it and the scale models are really lifelike. however, the ladders are a bit flimsy and can break easily. just a heads-up if you have rough players!

I was excited about this dollhouse for my daughter, but some of the furniture was missing. I contacted customer service, and they were quick to help me out. the design is fantastic, but I do wish quality control was a bit more reliable.

the details in this dollhouse are fantastic. I’m picky about interior decoration, and this one satisfies my aesthetic needs. however, the assembly is more complex than I anticipated. took about two hours to get everything set up right.

this is the cutest dollhouse! I love how it looks like a modern house with all the right furniture. but I did notice that the windows are a bit loose, which makes me worry they might fall out if played with too roughly. great design but just needs a little tweak!

I was worried this dollhouse wouldn't hold my kids' attention, but they love it! the furniture is very detailed and easy to move around. one thing I’ve noticed, though, is that the colors fade a bit after a few weeks in the sun. just be mindful if you keep it by a window.

my daughter has been playing with this dollhouse nonstop for almost a month. love the miniature furniture, especially the cute little dining set! but some corners of the dollhouse are a bit sharp, so we have to supervise her closely.

the architectural design is beautiful, and my kids love it! but, there are parts that feel a bit delicate. one of the railings broke off after just a week. I’m hoping it lasts longer because it really is a lovely piece.
I bought this for my granddaughter, and she was ecstatic. the scale models are so cute! however, I do wish the assembly instructions were clearer; I ended up putting one wall on upside down.

this dollhouse has detailed rooms that really spark creativity! my son plays with it every day. the only downside? some of the miniature furniture pieces are quite small and can easily get lost. a better storage solution would be ideal!

we've had this for a couple of months now, and it’s been great for imaginative play! the interior decoration is well thought out. however, the doors are a little tight and require some effort to open. my kid gets frustrated sometimes.

I love the vintage look of this dollhouse. the furniture is charming and adds to the overall aesthetic. on the flip side, the paint started chipping a bit after a few weeks of play. still a cute piece, though!

this dollhouse is a hit with my kids! they love the variety of rooms and the furniture. I did notice that some edges were rough, which isn’t great for younger kids. a little sanding would make it perfect.

my daughter is obsessed with this dollhouse! the miniature furniture adds a lovely touch to her playtime. however, I found a few pieces didn’t fit quite right, and it can be a bit frustrating when she’s trying to set things up.

I was a bit skeptical about this dollhouse at first, but my daughter adores it! the scale models are super cute, and she loves decorating them. that said, the assembly was a pain; there were so many tiny screws to deal with!

the architectural features of this dollhouse are stunning! my kids love the intricate design. unfortunately, the stairs are pretty wobbly, which worries me during playtime. overall, it's a great product but could use a little improvement.
these nail clippers are so sharp! they cut through my thick nails like butter. only downside is that they sometimes slip outta my hand if my hands are wet. a rubber grip would’ve made them perfect.
the cuticle pushers have a nice ergonomic handle, which makes using them less of a chore. however, the metal ends could be a bit sharper. sometimes I end up using a bit more force than I’d like.
got this nail file set about a month ago and I’m really impressed! they’re sturdy and don’t wear down quickly. but the packaging was super flimsy and one of the files arrived bent. that was annoying.
honestly, I was skeptical about this nail polish remover but it works pretty well! it takes a few extra swipes for darker colors, but at least it doesn't smell as strong as others I've tried.
the nail clippers work great for my kids' tiny nails. I love the safety features, but I wish they’d come in brighter colors! the one we have is super dull and boring.
just got this metal cuticle pusher, and it’s way better than the plastic ones. took a bit to get used to, but now I can't go back! just need to be careful not to push too hard.
these nail files are perfect for shaping! I use them for both my natural nails and acrylics. downside? they’re a bit bulky to fit in my travel kit, so I might need a smaller option.
I’ve been trying to grow my nails out, and this cuticle remover is a treat! it softens them really well. however, I noticed that it can be a bit too oily and leaves a residue.
the nail polish remover is alright. it gets the job done but feels a bit harsh on my nails, especially after frequent use. maybe I need to look for a gentler formula next time.
got this set of nail tools and I love the variety! the cuticle scissors are super sharp but I do have to be careful not to clip too much. it’s easy to get carried away!
these clippers are sturdy and surprisingly lightweight. I appreciate the built-in nail catcher, though it could be a bit deeper; I still end up with some clippings on the floor. yikes!
the glass nail file is totally worth it! smooths out rough edges without any fuss. just wish it came with a case, since I’m nervous about it breaking in my bag.
my gal pals swear by this nail polish remover and I finally tried it. it’s not too bad! but I still find myself going back to my old favorite because it feels less drying.
the cuticle nippers are amazing! they cut cleanly and don’t pull at my skin. just wish they had a locking mechanism; I have to be careful when storing them away.
I loved the design of these nail clippers, but honestly, they just don’t cut that well. I had to go back to my old clippers after struggling with them for weeks.
been using these for a couple of months now, and they hold up pretty well. the nail files aren’t the best for fine detailing though. I need to go back and get a finer grit.
I appreciate how well the cuticle pushers help in maintaining my manicure. they glide nicely over my nails but I wish they were a bit sturdier; one of mine bent after a few uses.
the portability of this nail file is fantastic! but it wears down faster than I expected. it’s also a bit too big to fit in my pocket comfortably, which is a bummer.
these clippers are a game changer! I finally feel like I can take care of my nails at home. however, I wish they had a more sleek design; they feel a bit bulky in my hand.
I really wanted to love this set of nail tools, but the cuticle scissors were dull right out of the box. I’ve had better luck with cheaper brands, which is disappointing.
the nail polish remover is decent and works fast. the scent is surprisingly mild too! but the bottle is way too small for the price; I feel like I’m always needing more.
the nail file set is a solid buy! I like how they come in different grits. one downside is that they aren’t very durable; I’ve gone through two already in less than a month.
these clippers are surprisingly great! they cut cleanly and have a nice grip. just be careful because they seem to rust a bit if you don’t wipe them off after use.
the glass nail file is my favorite find! it’s so gentle and doesn’t leave behind splintered edges. my only complaint is that it’s a bit fragile, so I always handle it with care.
I love the variety in this nail tool set! but honestly, the cuticle remover seems less effective compared to other brands I've used. still, the scissors are sharp and amazing.
overall, this nail clipper is efficient and does the job. however, I do wish it had a built-in nail cleaner like my old one. still, can’t complain for the price!

These little maracas are such a hit with my 1-year-old! They’re easy to hold, and my baby loves shaking them to make noise. I do wish they were a bit sturdier since one of them had a slight rattle after only a few weeks. Overall, they’re fun and keep my little one entertained for quite a while!

The Bluetooth speaker is super handy for playing lullabies. It connects easily to my phone, which is great. However, the battery life could be better; it only lasts about 4 hours on a full charge. Still, the sound is crystal clear, and my toddler loves it!

I really wanted my 2-year-old to enjoy music, so I got the toddler guitar. It has fun sounds and lights, but the strings are a bit too tight for little fingers. He has fun with it but tends to get frustrated. A bit more user-friendly would be nice!

These headphones fit my 3-year-old perfectly! They’re cute and come in bright colors. The volume limit feature is a great safety touch. However, the padding isn’t as thick as I’d hoped, so they can get a bit uncomfortable after long use. Still, overall good.

So I got the mini keyboard for my 18-month-old, and it's been fantastic! The different instrument sounds are really fun, and the lights are mesmerizing. That said, the keys aren't very responsive, so sometimes it requires a bit of pressure to get a sound out. It’s a mixed bag but enjoyable overall.

I purchased the music bug for my baby, and it’s adorable! It plays various tunes and has a dance mode that makes my 2-year-old giggle. The only downside is the volume can be a tad loud for my taste, but at least it has an off button!”,

these sustain eye drops have been a lifesaver for my dry eyes! i work long hours in front of a computer, and these drops really keep my eyes feeling fresh. they’re super hydrating and don’t have that sticky feeling like some others. my only issue? the bottle design makes it a bit tricky to get the last few drops out.

i've been using these eye vitamins for about 2 months now and while i think my vision is slightly improved, it’s hard to say for sure. they’re easy to swallow and don’t have a weird aftertaste, which is a plus! but honestly, i wish they were a bit cheaper since they are $25 for a month’s supply.

my husband needed prescription glasses and we ordered these cute frames. he loves them! they fit well and look great on him. the only downside is that the lenses took a little longer to arrive than we expected, about 2 weeks, but it was worth the wait for this stylish pair!

tried out these lubricating eye drops for my contacts and they work well enough. they provide relief, but i had to reapply them a few times throughout the day. i love that they come in a pack of 6, great for keeping one in my bag and by the bedside. just wish they lasted a bit longer.

recently started using these blue light blocking glasses while working at home, and i have to say they really do reduce eye strain. the design is sleek and super comfy. but, they can get a bit smudgy and need frequent cleaning, which is a bit annoying when i’m in a hurry.

if you wear contacts, these drops are amazing! they keep everything feeling fresh without any burning or irritation. just wish they were a little more affordable since i tend to go through them quickly and they’re about $15 a bottle.

got these eyeglasses for my 12-year-old daughter, and she adores them! the color is super fun and they fit her face perfectly. however, i noticed the lenses scratch easily, so we have to be careful. still, can't beat the price for how cute they are.

these omega-3 eye supplements have really helped with my dry eye issues lately. i’m on my second bottle now, and I can feel the difference for sure. just wish they didn’t have that fishy aftertaste sometimes! other than that, they’re great.

invested in these contact lens cleaning solutions and it’s made a huge difference. my lenses feel so much cleaner! however, the bottle is slightly bulky and takes up a lot of space in my bag, which isn’t ideal when traveling.

i am super picky with eye drops, but these worked wonders for my allergies! they relieve itching and redness quickly, but i found they can be a bit too heavy. made my eyes feel a little weighed down after a while.

initially, I was skeptical about these eye vitamins, but they really do help with fatigue! i’ve noticed my eyes feel less strained after long days. the only downside is the size of the pills; they’re a bit large and hard to swallow for some people.

absolutely love these reading glasses! they’re chic and perfect for my style. however, they came with a slight scratch on one lens which was disappointing. i’ve since learned to check for that before wearing them out.

these eye drops have been a lifesaver after staring at screens all day. they give instant relief! my only issue is that the bottle can be a little hard to squeeze without overdoing it, but still worth it for how good they feel.

found these daily disposable contacts and they’ve changed my life! no more cleaning them every night. just pop them in and out. only drawback is they are pricier in the long run, but the convenience is hard to beat.

ive been using these blue light glasses for a few weeks and honestly, they’re cute and reduce my headaches from screens. but, they tend to slip down my nose a bit too much. might try a different style next time.

wasn't sure about these eye drops at first, but they really do keep my eyes moist all day long! the only issue is the bottle cap; it’s a little flimsy and doesn’t always click back into place securely.
these contact lenses are super comfortable for daily wear! i often forget i have them in. however, they sometimes get foggy after several hours, which can be quite annoying.
the glasses arrived quickly, and they look fantastic! however, they hurt my ears a bit after wearing them too long. definitely need to adjust the arms, but loving the style overall.
oh man, these eye drops really soothe my allergies, but they do have a slight sting on application. also, the bottle is pretty small for the price, but it’s effective at flushing out irritants.
these readers are perfect for my age, stylish and lightweight. my only complaint? they feel a bit fragile. I’m really careful not to drop them, as I fear they’ll break easily.
just started using these eye vitamins and they’re reasonable in price, but I haven’t seen any noticeable difference yet. i'm going to give them a few more months to see if anything changes.
i love these eye drops! they’re hydrating and work wonders with my contacts. but the bottle design makes it hard to get the last few drops out, which is frustrating.
got these sunglasses as a pair of backup glasses. they’re surprisingly comfortable but don't fit my face perfectly. they slip a little, but they’re pretty cute overall.
the contact solution works great but i have to say the size of the bottle isn't ideal for travel. I wish they had a smaller travel-size option available!
my daughter needs glasses and these ones are perfect! they fit well and look adorable on her. the only problem is the case it came with wasn’t the best quality.
purchased these eye vitamins on a whim, and while I can't see a drastic change, my eyes feel a bit less strained during long screen hours. might repurchase if they stay affordable!
these contact lenses are life-changing! so comfortable and breathable. just wish they weren't a bit pricey, but you get what you pay for in comfort.

this pill organizer has been a lifesaver for my mom who takes multiple medications daily. the compartments are a perfect size and it’s easy to open. however, i wish the lid closed more securely. she’s accidentally knocked it over a couple of times and spilled her pills everywhere. overall, a solid buy!

after trying a few different pill splitters, this one really stands out! it splits my larger vitamins evenly and easily. very sharp blade! only downside is that it feels a bit flimsy, so i’m not sure how long it will last. still, it’s worth the price!

love this portable pill case! small enough to fit in my purse but spacious enough for a week’s worth of meds. it’s made my on-the-go life so much easier. just wish the colors were more fun; it’s pretty basic looking.

honestly, i have mixed feelings about this medication organizer. while it holds a good amount and the compartments are clearly labeled, the lid doesn’t snap shut very tightly. i’ve found it open in my bag before, so just be careful!
