after using this sunscreen, I feel protected and moisturized at the same time! however, it can get a bit shiny on my oily skin, especially in the summer heat. it’s still a solid pick for sun protection, though!
this product is great for all-day wear, and it has a pleasant scent. however, it can feel a bit thick when applying. once it soaks in, it’s great for a day at the park!

picked this sunscreen for my honeymoon in Hawaii and it worked wonders. no sunburn at all! but I wish the bottle was more travel-friendly; it took up space in my bag. still, super happy with the results!

these disposable cups are super sturdy! we used them for my daughter's birthday party and they held up great even with hot drinks. no leaks or spills. only downside is they can't be recycled, so that feels a bit wasteful. but for convenience, they were perfect!

got these trash bags for my kitchen and they fit my can perfectly. they hold a good amount without tearing which is great. my only issue is that they smell a bit funky when you first open the box, which was kinda off-putting. overall, they do the job though.

these plastic utensils were a lifesaver for our picnic last weekend! no bending or breaking, and they looked decent enough for a family gathering. my only complaint is the forks were a bit flimsy when it came to harder foods. but for the price, I can’t complain too much.

the food storage bags are nice quality. they zip well and keep my leftovers fresh. I use them for marinating meats too, and I've had no leaks. wish they came in smaller sizes though, sometimes I have just a tiny bit of food that doesn't fit in these larger bags.

I picked up these disposable cups for a casual get-together, and while they were functional, I noticed a few had small cracks right out of the box. it’s a decent option, but I’m hesitant to buy them again unless I can find a better brand.

these trash bags have been perfect for my household needs. I’ve used them for over a month and not one has ripped on me. they could be a bit thicker but honestly, they do hold quite a bit of trash. I will buy again for sure.

the food storage bags are okay. they seal pretty well but I had a couple that didn’t hold up with liquids. I ended up with a mess in the fridge. but for dry snacks and things, they work just fine. just be cautious if you're storing soup or anything.

I love these plastic utensils for kids’ parties. they’re colorful and fun but slightly lighter than I expected, which means they bend if kids get too enthusiastic with their food. so I have to watch them a bit. still, they're a great choice for a hassle-free meal!

these cups are surprisingly thick! used them for both hot coffee and cool drinks at my family BBQ, and they performed like champs. the only downside is they aren't very eco-friendly, but for a one-time use, they did great for me.

was impressed with the durability of these food storage bags! I’ve had them in the freezer for weeks now, and none have popped or leaked. but I wish the sliding zip was easier to use; sometimes it's a bit tricky to get it closed right.

the trash bags are decent for the price but I wish they had a scent or something. they do the job, but an odor-masking feature would be nice. also, they can fit a lot more than I expected, which is a big plus.

got these disposable cups for my outdoor party. while they held up well with cold drinks, I wouldn’t trust them with something really hot. I ended up using my good mugs for tea, so just keep that in mind if you're serving anything steaming.

these plastic utensils came in handy for a family reunion. they’re sturdy enough for most foods, but I found the spoons to be a bit on the small side. overall, a good buy though! just wish the spoon size was better for eating salad.

the food storage bags are good quality and super easy to write on for labeling. I like that they come in multiple sizes. however, I sometimes find them hard to open quickly, especially when I'm in a rush.

I liked these disposable cups for the price, but they were a bit smaller than I expected. perfect for kids, but adults wanted larger servings. still, they did the job for our family BBQ, and I appreciate how lightweight they are!

I was looking forward to these trash bags since I read good reviews, but I found them a bit thin. one tore when I was taking it out which was a bummer. they hold a lot, but I wish they were a tad thicker for sturdiness.

the plastic utensils are colorful and fun, perfect for kids! but I found the forks to bend when trying to stab firmer foods like meat. they were great for pasta and salad, though. might stick to using them just for lighter fare.

these food storage bags are okay for the price. they keep things fresh but the sealing isn’t always reliable. I've had a couple leaks and I was disappointed. still, they are convenient for everyday use, just be careful with liquids.

I bought these cups for a picnic and they worked well for drinks and snacks, no leaks at all! just wish they had a more sophisticated design. they look a bit cheap, but hey, they’re disposable! overall, satisfied with my purchase.

these trash bags are the best I've found! no ripping, and they fit my standard can perfectly. they hold up with heavy loads too, which is amazing. the only thing is they can be a bit too scented for my taste, but I’ll take the trade-off.

the plastic utensils are pretty standard. I like the variety of colors they come in, but they do feel a bit flimsy compared to others I’ve used. they work for salads and soft foods, but I wouldn’t trust them for anything else.

I used these food storage bags during meal prep and they were fantastic! they sealed really well, no spills in the fridge. only thing is, they could be a bit bigger for storing larger meals. overall, I'm happy with them.

these disposable cups were a hit at my daughter’s baby shower! I loved the variety of colors. they were a bit smaller than I pictured, so plan accordingly if your guests are heavy drinkers. great for punch and snacks, though!

tried these trash bags, and they’re decent but not the best. they hold a lot, but I found a few tearing when I was loading them up. not exactly reliable, but for light trash, they’ll do just fine. wouldn’t buy them again.

these food storage bags are a solid choice! I love how they stand up by themselves - makes filling them so much easier. but I’ve noticed the closure isn’t as tight as I’d like; I had a minor leak on one occasion. mostly satisfied, though!

these depend adult diapers are a lifesaver for my dad who is 78 and has mobility issues. they fit well and keep him comfortable throughout the day. only downside is that they can feel a bit bulky under clothes, but the absorbency is fantastic. he's had no leaks, and that's all that matters for us! about $30 for a pack of 30 is a decent deal too.

bought a pack of these incontinence briefs for my mom after her surgery, and she says they are super soft and don’t irritate her skin at all. they fit her well, but I noticed the tabs could be a bit stronger since they sometimes pop open when she moves around. overall, she’s happy, which makes me happy!

not my favorite. the bladder control pads don’t stay in place very well and can shift if you're active. my grandma wore them for a family gathering and ended up feeling uncomfortable. the absorbency is decent, but I think I’ll try a different brand next time.

got these overnight absorbent undergarments for my husband, and they do the job. he sleeps through the night without worrying about leaks. however, they could use a little more stretch; they feel a bit tight around the thighs. still, they’re much better than others we’ve tried!

really impressed with these bladder control pads! they provide solid protection without feeling bulky. I’ve been using them for about two weeks now, and I appreciate that they’re discreet. only con? they are slightly pricey at around $25 for a box of 50. but in my opinion, worth it.

the absorbent undergarments are okay, but they could improve on the odor control. they do a good job absorbing but sometimes the smell is a bit strong, especially after a long day. I’ve been using them for about a month, and I think I might look for something more breathable next time.

these adult diapers have been so helpful for my father, who has late-stage Alzheimer's. they fit great and he hasn’t had any leaks. my only complaint is the price, which tends to be higher than I’d like at around $40 for a pack of 32. but honestly, peace of mind is priceless.

picked these incontinence briefs for my sister who’s recovering from childbirth. she loves how comfortable they are and says they give her the coverage she needs. the only issue is that they don’t come in a variety of sizes; she’s a little on the smaller side, so they sag a bit.

the absorbent undergarments are not as absorbent as I expected. on light days, they're fine, but I had a mishap when I thought I could rely on them overnight. I think I'll stick to the brand I was using before, even if these are cheaper.

my husband has been using these bladder control pads for about six months, and they’re a solid go-to for him. they're thin, which he loves, but the stickiness could be better. sometimes they don’t hold as well as I’d like, especially when he’s up and about.

the depend adult diapers worked wonders during my trip last month. we drove for hours and they kept me dry. my only issue is they can be a tad noisy when moving around. made for some awkward moments at rest stops, haha!

these incontinence briefs are great for my aunt, who is in her 70s. very soft and easy to put on. but, I wish they had more absorbency; she has had a couple of leaks during the night. still, a bit better than the other brands we’ve tried.

tried these bladder control pads after hearing so many good things about them and I'm on the fence. they absorb well enough but do bunch up when I move. I’ve had better luck with a different brand but will give these another chance.

these absorbent undergarments are decent. they do their job without leaks! however, they could be more breathable. I often feel hot and sticky if I wear them for long stretches, especially when I’m active.
was skeptical about these adult diapers, but they really surprised me! very comfortable and discreet. downside is the sizing runs a bit large, so I’d recommend sizing down if you're in between sizes.

these bladder control pads are a decent option, but they’re not as thin as I hoped. they absorb well, but I feel like I'm wearing a diaper sometimes. probably won’t buy again, just want something less bulky.

I have mixed feelings about these incontinence briefs. On one hand, they absorb really well; on the other hand, they sometimes leak at the sides if I'm not careful. I’d probably continue using them since I haven't found anything better yet.

the depend adult diapers worked great for my husband after his surgery. they protected him well, but he did complain about them feeling a little rough on the skin. might consider trying a different brand next time!

these absorbent undergarments offer great protection but have a strong plastic smell when you first open the package. I was worried it would bother me, but it faded after a day. They fit well and I've had no leaks, so that’s a win.

after trying these bladder control pads, I have to say they held up better than expected! They’re very discreet. That said, they did stick to my skin a bit, and I had to adjust them a few times throughout the day.
I bought these adult diapers for my uncle, and although they fit well, he said they made him feel a little hot. They do a good job of keeping him dry but I think he’d prefer something a bit cooler.
the absorbent undergarments are pretty reliable, but I wish they had better odor control. I’d rate them higher if that was improved. They do fit nicely and stay in place for the most part, though.

these incontinence briefs are a hit for my sister-in-law! they fit great and provide a lot of comfort, but she did mention they felt a little pricey at $35 for just 30. Quality is solid, but budget-friendly options would be nice.
I’m really pleased with these bladder control pads! They fit nicely and are super absorbent. However, I do wish they came in a larger package; I only got 40 for about $25. Going through them quickly!
the depend adult diapers are good, but I wish they weren’t so bulky. They work well without leaks, but I feel like I have to wear loose fitting clothing, which can be a hassle.

these absorbent undergarments are a decent choice for daytime use but were less than stellar overnight. I woke up to a leak after a long night. They’re comfortable, but I’m on the hunt for something more reliable for bedtime.

this shampoo has been a lifesaver for my dry scalp! after just a few washes, my hair felt so much cleaner and less itchy. i love the lather it produces, honestly feels like a spa. however, i wish the scent lasted a bit longer, it’s quite light and disappears quickly.

i've been using this shampoo for about a month now, and it really does a great job at cleansing without stripping my hair. my curls have been looking bouncy! but the price is a bit steep for my budget, around $25 for a small bottle. can’t decide if it’s worth the splurge yet.

not the best for my oily hair. it didn’t dry out my scalp, which is great, but my hair still felt greasy by the end of the day. the scent is pleasant, though, very fresh! i might try it once more just to give it another shot.

i got this shampoo for my 5-year-old son who has super sensitive skin. he hasn’t reacted at all, which makes me so relieved! the cleansing agents seem gentle yet effective, but i wish it lathered a bit more. sometimes it feels like i’m not getting enough suds for all that hair.

i was really excited to try this shampoo after reading rave reviews. it definitely cleans well and my hair feels so soft afterward! but, the bottle is kind of small for the price, so i run out quickly. also, it does make my hair a little tangled after rinsing.

great shampoo for scalp care! i’ve struggled with dandruff for years, and this really helps keep it in check without making my hair feel like straw. the flip side is that i have to use a lot to get enough lather. for the price, i kinda wish it came in a bigger size.

love this shampoo! been using it for about 6 weeks now and my hair feels so shiny and vibrant. the lather is fantastic. just wish it didn’t come with that weird flip top; it's hard to get the last bits out of it. like, come on, make it easier to use!

this shampoo has some seriously good cleansing agents. my scalp feels refreshed and clean after every wash. the only downside is that it's a bit on the thicker side, which makes it a little hard to spread evenly through my hair. apart from that, no complaints!

this shampoo was recommended by my hairstylist and wow, I can see why! my hair feels healthy and has a great bounce. the only con is that it’s a little pricey for a regular shampoo, around $30, but I guess you get what you pay for.

wasn't super impressed with this shampoo. it cleans okay, but my hair felt kind of dull afterward. the scent is nice, like fresh flowers, but it doesn’t last. for the price I expected a bit more zing. might not repurchase after finishing this bottle.

my hair feels sooo soft after using this! I love that it has natural ingredients and no sulfates. the lather is decent, though it could be a bit more bubbly for my taste. definitely a bit pricier, around $28, but worth it for the results so far!

I have fine hair and this shampoo really cleans without weighing it down. it's been about 2 months, and I still love it! however, I wish it had a stronger scent to enjoy while washing. overall, it’s been a solid addition to my hair care routine.

the consistency of this shampoo is a little runny, which threw me off at first. it does cleanse well, but I feel like I end up using more than I’d like. it's great for my scalp, though, no more itching! just wish it came in a larger bottle.

I had high hopes for this shampoo, but it just didn't work for my hair type. loved the idea of the natural ingredients, but my hair felt heavy and greasy afterward. maybe it works better for others, but I’ll be looking for something lighter next time.

I bought this on a whim and boy, what a surprise! my hair feels so clean, and the frizz is manageable. the only issue? it’s quite expensive for the amount you get. i’m trying to ration it now because it’s just about $35! might be worth it in the end, though.

after trying countless shampoos, I finally found one that helps with my flaky scalp. I’ve been using it for 3 weeks, and my scalp feels much better. that being said, it’s a little drying on the hair. I usually need a good conditioner afterward.

I really wanted to like this shampoo! it does cleanse really well and leaves my hair smelling fresh, but it also makes my hair feel a bit dry. I have to pair it with a heavy conditioner to bring back some moisture. might not be great for dry hair types.

this shampoo has lovely packaging and smells divine! the lather is nice too, but I didn't find it to be super cleansing. I felt like I had to wash twice to get the desired effect. still, it did make my hair feel soft afterward.

I’ve been using this shampoo for a month now and while it does a decent job of cleaning, I’m finding that it makes my hair a bit frizzy. It’s great for scalp health, but I might have to switch it up for a more moisturizing product.

absolutely love this for my colored hair! it keeps my color vibrant and doesn’t fade it, which is hard to find. however, it doesn’t lather as much as I’d like. it might be due to the cleansing agents, but I can deal with that for the extra shine!

this shampoo smells awesome and cleans really well, but I found it hard to lather on my thick hair. I’d say it’s a good pick for finer hair types but I’ll need something more moisturizing since my hair ends up tangled.

I’ve been using this for about 2 months now, and I can see a difference in my scalp health. my dandruff has reduced significantly! the downside? it can feel a bit heavy if I use too much. I have to watch how much I squeeze out.

the ingredients in this shampoo really caught my attention. they were supposed to help balance my oily scalp, and they did! but, it can leave my ends feeling dry if I don’t follow up with a good conditioner. that’s my trade-off.

I’ve tried a lot of shampoos for my sensitive scalp, and this one is a winner! no irritation at all. however, I was hoping for more lather; it doesn’t foam as much as I like. overall, though, I’m happy with the results!

pretty decent shampoo but not a miracle worker. my hair felt cleaner after use, but it didn’t really do anything for my frizz. might be good for someone with straighter hair. still on the lookout for the perfect shampoo for my waves!

this shampoo delivers a gentle cleanse and smells amazing! my hair feels soft and manageable. my only gripe is the size for the price; it’s a bit small. I feel like I’ll run out before I’m ready to buy again, which is a bummer.

this karaoke machine is a hit in our household! the dual microphone feature is perfect for my kids to sing duets. they love the vocal effects too, it makes them feel like real stars. the song library is impressively large, covering almost all their favorites. only downside is it sometimes struggles to connect to our wifi. overall, a great buy for family fun!

i got this for my 8-year-old's birthday and she hasn't stopped using it. the microphone stand is sturdy and adjustable, which is great since she’s still growing. the sound quality is pretty decent for kids, but I wish it had a better bass. for about $50, it’s a solid option, but I think there are better choices out there.

what a fun gadget! my 5-year-old loves it. the song library has a lot of children's songs, and the vocal effects keep her engaged. however, I noticed the microphone doesn’t always pick up soft singing. still, we have a blast with it. just make sure to keep the volume at a reasonable level to avoid neighbors complaining!

my kids have been using this karaoke player for about 3 months and it's been a success at playdates! the fact that it has two microphones is a huge plus, they love to sing together. it does freeze up occasionally which is frustrating. overall, it’s entertaining and keeps them busy, just wish it was a bit more reliable.

we bought this karaoke system for family game night, and it has brought so much laughter! the vocal effects are silly and make everyone crack up. however, the song library could use more current pop songs. for a kid's product, it's really fun, but I find myself wishing for some classics.

my daughter is obsessed with singing, so this was a perfect gift. the microphone stand is great, but I do wish it was a bit taller for her as she gets older. the sound quality is impressive, way better than I expected for an entry-level karaoke machine. still, I can't help but feel like the interface is a little clunky.

we've had this karaoke machine for a few weeks now, and it’s been a mixed bag. the kids are having a blast, but the song library lacks some popular hits. the dual microphone option is great for singing battles! I love that it’s easy to set up, but I wish the battery life was longer. overall, fun but could use some improvements!

my teen loves this karaoke player! she uses it almost daily to practice her singing. the vocal effects are really neat and add some flair. however, the microphone doesn’t seem to work well at a distance, which is a bit annoying during performances. decent product for the price, just keep it close when singing!

bought this for my son’s birthday party, and it was a hit! the kids loved the dual microphone feature. the music selection is great, even though there are a few songs missing. I’m a bit disappointed that the speaker quality could be better; sometimes it sounds a little muddy.

this karaoke system is amazing for kids. my 6-year-old loves using it with her friends. I appreciate how user-friendly it is. the only thing is, the microphone cable feels a bit thin and fragile, I'm worried it might break easily. but so far, we’ve had a lot of fun with it!

the karaoke machine is fun and keeps my kids entertained for hours. I love the variety in the song library. we have been using it for about 2 months. the only issue is that the Bluetooth connectivity can be a bit spotty at times. if that could be fixed, it would be perfect!

I got this karaoke machine mainly for my 10-year-old. the adjustable microphone stand is fantastic and really helps with comfort during singing. however, the vocal effects can be hit or miss; some of them sound a bit cheesy. for the price point, it’s worth it, but don’t expect a super professional setup.

the karaoke player is entertaining, but I wish the song library was updated more often. my kids have found some favorites, but they've already sung them all! the dual microphone is a nice touch, though. it seems pretty durable, and the kids have dropped it a couple of times without any issues.

we've been using this karaoke machine for family nights, and it's a lot of fun! the sound quality is quite good compared to other kids' electronics. I love that it has a built-in speaker, but I wish the volume could go a little louder. overall, worth the investment for the fun it brings.

my children adore this karaoke player! the large song library keeps them entertained, especially during long car rides. we’ve had it for about 4 months now. that said, the microphone quality could use some improvement. sometimes it sounds a little tinny. still, they’re having a blast with it!

this karaoke machine is perfect for family gatherings! the dual microphone feature makes it great for sing-alongs, though it sometimes takes a while to connect via Bluetooth. the kids really love the vocal effects, it makes them laugh. for the price, it’s a lot of fun, just wish the setup was a bit more straightforward.

I bought this product for my daughter who loves to sing. the karaoke machine has a good variety of songs, but I think it could benefit from more options. the vocal effects are fun to use, but they’re not always perfect. overall, it’s entertaining, just doesn’t completely fit my expectations.

the karaoke player has been a fun addition to our home! my kids love the song library and have so much fun with the effects. however, the user interface could be easier to navigate, especially for younger kids. I’m happy with the purchase, but it could use a little refinement.

this karaoke setup is so cool! my kids can’t get enough of it. the adjustable microphone stand is fantastic and works well for any height. however, I noticed the Bluetooth range isn’t great—it disconnects if they wander too far away. still, it's a good value overall for the fun it offers!

this karaoke machine is a good choice for young kids. the song selection is fun and they enjoy the vocal effects. I've had it for about a month, and the only downside is that the sound quality could be better. it’s good for the price, but I’ve heard better quality from other brands.

this has been a perfect gift for my kids! they love the dual microphone feature and spend hours singing together. however, I noticed that the battery life isn’t very long. we have to charge it frequently, which can be annoying during parties. still, the fun they have with it makes up for it!

the karaoke machine is enjoyable; however, the setup was a bit challenging for me at first. the kids have adored it since day one! they can't get enough of the effects, but sometimes they wish there were more songs. overall, it’s good value for the fun factor, just needs some more updates.

my daughter is 7 and absolutely loves this karaoke machine! the song library is pretty extensive, but it does lack some current pop songs. the dual microphone is great for singing duets; however, I've noticed it can be sensitive to volume levels, which makes it tricky during loud performances. but overall, it's been a fun addition to our home!

These bath bombs are so vibrant and colorful! I love how they fizz and release the scent of lavender, making my bath feel like a spa day. However, they can leave a bit of residue in the tub, which is a pain to clean up. Just keep that in mind when you’re done relaxing!

I recently tried the bubble bath from this brand, and it really does create the largest bubbles I've ever seen! My kids had a blast, but the scent was a little too strong for my taste. It's nice for a fun bath time, but I wouldn't use it every night.

Bath salts from this brand are okay, but they don’t dissolve as quickly as I'd like. I usually have to stir them a bit to get them to melt completely. They do have a lovely eucalyptus scent, and my muscles feel relaxed afterwards, so I guess that's a win! Just wish they were easier to use.

These bath oils are divine! I’ve used them for a few weeks and my skin feels super moisturized afterward. The only downside is that they do make the tub a bit slippery, so be cautious when getting out. Otherwise, they're a wonderful treat.

Every night, I treat myself with these jasmine bath bombs. They smell amazing and turn the water such a beautiful pink! My only complaint is that they don’t create much fizz, but they sure do make my skin feel soft and smell great.

I was excited about these bubble bath products, but they didn’t make as many bubbles as I expected. They do smell nice and clean, which is a plus, but I won’t be buying them again if I’m looking for a bubbly experience.

Bath salts are a must-have in my self-care routine, and these are quite nice! They smell fresh, and I find myself feeling relaxed after soaking. But they don't seem to dissolve as well as some others I've tried. Still, I like them overall.

After trying several brands, this bath bomb set is definitely one of my favorites! They’re packed with essential oils and really help me unwind. Just a heads up, they tend to leave color in the tub, but it's worth it for the relaxation.

I bought the bubble bath for my daughter, and it's a hit! She loves adding them to her bath, and it makes for a fun experience. I wish the scent lasted longer, but for the price, it’s great! We're on our second bottle now.

These bath oils are incredible! A few drops leave my skin feeling silky smooth, and the scent of vanilla is so comforting. My only issue is that the bottle can be a bit tricky to dispense without spilling.

Tried this bubble bath during a pamper night and while it smelled lovely, I was sad to find that it didn’t produce as many bubbles as I hoped. It still made for a nice soak, but if you’re looking for something bubbly, keep searching.

I really enjoy these bath bombs! They fizz nicely and smell fantastic. However, I did find that they can stain my tub a bit, so I have to clean it after every use. Still, they make my bath special, so I might keep buying them!

The bath salts are soothing and help me unwind after a long day. However, I found they don’t dissolve completely, which is a bummer when you’re trying to relax. Still, for the price, I think they do the job well enough.

I’m a big fan of these jasmine bubble baths! The smell is heavenly and reminds me of spa days. Just wish they created a bit more foam, though. Overall, they’re great for a relaxing night in, just not for the bubbles.

I treated myself to some bath bombs from this brand and they were delightful! The smell was intoxicating, and I felt relaxed afterward. The downside? They leave a little color in the tub, so just be prepared for that when you clean up.

My kids love the bubble bath from this line! It gives off a light scent and makes bath time a blast, but I wish it wasn’t so hard to rinse off. Sometimes it leaves a film on their skin that I have to scrub away.

The bath oils I purchased left my skin feeling soft, but the scent was a bit too strong for my liking. I prefer something more subtle. They are nice for a treat, but I won’t use them every bath. Moderation is key!

I have sensitive skin and was hesitant to try these bath bombs, but they worked pretty well for me! The lavender scent is calming and not overpowering. Just be careful, too many at once can make my skin feel a bit oily.

I love the bath salts' scent, but they take a little longer to dissolve in the water than I expected. Still, the aroma fills the bathroom nicely, and I do feel relaxed after soaking. I might repurchase, just maybe a different scent.

I bought these bubble baths for my two-year-old, and she loves them! They create a decent amount of bubbles, which keeps her entertained. My only complaint is they don’t have a long-lasting scent, but for her, it's all about the fun!

I was excited to use these bath oils after hearing great things. While they did leave my skin feeling moisturized, I found the scent overwhelming. A little goes a long way, so I had to adjust how much I used. Still, a nice product overall.

I adore these bath bombs! Every bath feels like a luxury treat, but I do wish they were a bit cheaper. They do leave some little glitter bits behind, which I didn't expect. If you don’t mind cleaning up a bit, go for it!

The bubble bath does create a nice amount of foam; however, the scent doesn't last long in the water, which is kind of a letdown. It's a fun addition to bath time for my kids, but I’ll probably look for a stronger scent next time.

These bath salts do a great job of helping me relax after a long day. They smell great, but they seem to take forever to dissolve in the tub, which can be a bit annoying. Still, I enjoyed the experience overall.

I tried the bath oils and really liked how smooth they made my skin feel. However, they left a greasy residue on the tub, which wasn’t ideal. I guess I’ll just have to be more diligent about cleaning up after my pampering sessions.

These bath bombs smell delightful and are a fun addition to my baths! They can leave a bit of color in the tub, but that’s a small price to pay for relaxation. I would definitely buy them again for my self-care days.

this herbal essence coconut milk conditioner smells heavenly, honestly! my hair felt softer after just one wash, but it didn't do much for detangling. my hair is super thick, so i think it needs extra moisture. cost was about $8, but i might try a thicker one next time.

i love how moisturizing this shea moisture conditioner is! it really brings life back to my dry hair, and the fragrance is divine. however, it does leave my hair a bit greasy if i use too much. so be careful with the amount! overall, it’s about $11 well spent.

this garnier whole blends conditioner is a nice budget option. it made my hair feel smoother while styling, especially after a long day at work. my hair is wavy, but it felt a bit weighed down. it’s around $6, so i can't complain too much, but i am still on the hunt for something lighter.

i have highlighted hair, and this conditioner was perfect for keeping it nourished. my hair felt silky after using it! but, it didn’t quite help with the frizz as much as i’d hoped. for about $12, it's decent but not magical for my wavy locks.

this leave-in conditioner is a lifesaver for my ends. super moisturizing and hydrating! it makes detangling a breeze. but, it can be a bit heavy if you apply too much — learned that the hard way! about $15, and i’ll keep using it!

tried the amika soulfood nourishing mask and it's as good as people say! my curls were bouncy after just one use. i still had to use some extra product to tame the frizz though, so keep that in mind. it’s around $28, and i’ll probably buy it again.

not in love with the dove deep moisture conditioner. while it does hydrate well, my scalp felt itchy after a few uses. it’s super affordable at around $5, but i think i’ll switch to something sulfate-free next time.

this aussie 3-minute miracle conditioner is my go-to for a quick shower fix! it really does moisturize in just a few minutes, and my hair feels soft. only downside is the scent, which is a bit too sweet for my taste. about $3, so not bad for a quick fix!

the ojon restorative conditioner really helped repair my damaged ends. my hair felt much healthier and detangled easier. the only downside is the price — around $30 — but it's worth it if you're looking for something rich and nourishing.

was expecting big things from this tresemme conditioner after hearing so much hype. my hair was soft for sure, but it didn't seem to last as long as I hoped. at around $10, it does the job, but I might try others next time.

this botanical repair conditioner from Aveda is super hydrating! my hair felt amazing, but the price point is pretty high at about $35. if you're looking for a luxury treatment, it’s worth it, but I might save it for special occasions.

the moroccanoil hydrating conditioner is a total treat! really nourishes without weighing my hair down. the downside — it's a bit pricey at $26. if you can splurge, it’s great, but if you're on a budget, look for alternatives.

i'm in love with the biolage hydrasource conditioner! makes my hair super soft and manageable. it’s about $20, which is not cheap, but it lasts a long time! only downside is it didn’t help with my flyaways much.

just started using the loreal elvive total repair and my hair feels so much healthier! really great for dry ends. one thing i noticed though, it can get a bit greasy if I use too much. at around $8, it’s nice to have in my rotation!

the suave professionals almond and shea butter conditioner is surprisingly nice for the price! about $4 for a big bottle, and it moisturizes well enough. just wish it detangled better; I still struggle with knots.

this redken extreme conditioner is good for strength and moisture! after using it for a few weeks, my hair feels stronger, but sometimes it feels a bit heavy. priced around $20, I feel it’s a solid investment for damaged hair.

treat yourself to the bridgewater nourishing conditioner! smells like a dream and makes my hair super soft, but it's about $22, which is steep! still, I love it for my once-in-a-while pampering sessions.

the herbal essence bio:renew is a nice find! my hair was shiny and smooth after one use. but it didn’t work quite as well on my tangles. for $8, I’d say it’s a decent value, but I want more detangling power.

while the garnier sleek and shine was okay, I didn’t find it as moisturizing as I hoped. it did help with frizz, but my ends still felt dry. around $6, so I may keep trying other options before committing long term.

the nature's gate conditioner did not wow me. while it claims to be hydrating, my hair felt dry after using it. I wanted to love it since it's around $9, but I think I’ll stick with more moisturizing brands moving forward.

I've been using OGX coconut milk conditioner for a while, and wow, it smells amazing! my hair feels soft, but sometimes I find it hard to rinse out completely. it’s around $10, so that’s not too bad, but I wish it rinsed out better!

the TRESemmé Botanique conditioner is pretty lovely! great for moisturizing and doesn't leave my hair too heavy. the downside? just a bit pricey for the size at about $15. still, I like the clean ingredients!

this cough syrup worked wonders for my 4-year-old. she was hacking all night, and after one dose, she finally slept. it’s not super thick like some others, which I liked, but the taste could use some improvement. she didn’t love it, but hey, it did the trick!

the chest congestion relief is decent, but I expected more for the price. it’s about $12 for a small bottle, and while it works okay, I still had to use my humidifier. I just wish it had more of a lasting effect. it helped, just not as much as I had hoped.

I really like this cold medicine! it’s gentle yet effective and doesn’t leave me feeling too drowsy. I have a busy schedule, so that’s key for me. but watch out, the taste is a bit weird. like, almost syrupy, not the best flavor for sure.

my husband caught a nasty flu bug, and this syrup seemed to help ease his cough. I’m not sure it’s a miracle, but it did alleviate some symptoms. I wish it would’ve acted faster, though. still, better than nothing, and he did appreciate the non-drowsy formula.

bought this for my 2-year-old after he came down with a cold. he hated taking it, but I can’t blame him, the taste is off! it did help a lot with his cough, but I had to chase him around to get him to take it. just wish they’d flavored it better!

this herbal cough syrup was a lifesaver during last winter’s flu season. I loved the natural ingredients, which made me feel better about giving it to my family. it worked really well on my chest congestion, but the texture was a little too thick for my liking.

not my favorite. I got this cold medicine thinking it would be a step up from the usual, but honestly, it didn’t do much. felt like I was just taking sugar water. I’ll stick to my regular stuff next time. it’s a shame because it got good reviews!

this is a solid cough syrup! my throat felt so much better after just one dose. I even noticed the coughing was less frequent. it’s a bit sticky, but I’ll take that for the relief it provided. just wish it came in a larger size, ran out too fast!

really impressed by this product! I had a nasty chest cold, and it cleared things up nicely without that groggy feeling. I was back to work within a couple of days. however, I wish the price was a bit lower; $15 is a tad steep for a single bottle.

I’ve tried a dozen cold medicines, and this one didn’t amaze me. it worked okay, but I still had a runny nose and achey body. the flavor wasn’t bad, but nothing that would make me choose it again over something else. maybe it’s just me!

this flu remedy came through for me during a rough week! I was in bed for days, and this helped me finally get some relief. I still felt tired, but it was nice not to have that awful scratchy throat. it’s just a bit pricey, though; I spent about $25.

used this on my little one. it did help reduce his coughing, but getting him to take it was a battle. the flavor is kind of herbal, so he wasn’t a fan. still, I felt better knowing it was natural. I’d give it another go next time!

wow, this cough syrup really exceeded my expectations! my sister’s been coughing for days, and it calmed things down within hours. the only downside was the slightly strange aftertaste, but it’s worth it for how fast it worked.

this cough medicine had mixed results for me. it did ease my symptoms, but I still felt pretty lousy after taking it. I guess it helped with my cough but didn’t do much for my stuffy nose. could be better for the price point, honestly.

grabbed this for my son when he got a cold. he didn’t mind the flavor, which was a huge bonus! it worked well for his cough, but I had to make sure he drank plenty of water to keep things moving. overall, a good product!

I’ve used a lot of cold remedies, and this one is middle of the road. it didn’t hurt my symptoms, but it didn’t exactly knock my socks off either. it’s a decent choice, but I wouldn’t buy it again if I can find something cheaper that works just as well.

this syrup is a nice alternative to typical cough medicine. I love that it’s natural, and it did help with my cough and cold symptoms! only downside? it’s expensive! I spent nearly $18, which seems a bit much for such a small bottle.

found this amazing for relieving chest congestion! I was surprised how well it worked. however, I didn’t love the sticky texture of it. I guess the trade-off was worth it since I could finally breathe properly!

took this during my recent cold and it definitely helped. I coughed less, but my nose was still running like crazy. I guess it was fine, but I was hoping it would cover all bases. not bad if you’re looking for something specific.

just finished a bottle for the flu. worked okay but was a bit hard to swallow. I liked the natural ingredients, but it could’ve masked taste better. still, it helped reduce my coughing quite a bit, so I can’t complain too much.

this product had a decent effect on my cold symptoms but took longer to kick in than I expected. I was still feeling under the weather after a couple of days, so I might try something else next time. it’s worth a shot, but not amazing.

I love that this cough syrup is made from natural ingredients! helped ease my throat without the heavy feeling of other cough meds. I just wish it came with a better measuring cap; I spilled some when pouring it.

can’t say I’m in love with this one. it did some good for my cough, but I could still feel the congestion. for the price, I expected something more effective. it’s okay, but I wouldn’t rush to buy it again.
this chest congestion remedy is a lifesaver when I have a cold! it really helps me breathe easier. the downside? it’s not the most pleasant flavor, and I found myself grimacing every time I took it.

great cough syrup! helped me sleep through the night finally. the only downside is the bottle is so small for the price. I mean, I went through it in just a few days, and it cost me about $18. wish they had a bigger size!

a friend recommended this to me, and it worked wonderfully for her. I had mixed results; while it helped my cough, I still felt really congested. it’s okay, but I expected more. I might give it another shot later!

got this for my daughter who was really struggling with a bad cough. it didn’t taste great, but she took it without too much fuss. it worked pretty well, and I was impressed with how quickly it helped her feel better!

this cough relief was a big help during my cold, but the taste was a little hard to get through. I appreciated that it didn’t make me groggy though! just wish they’d find a better flavor to make it easier to take.

overall, this cold medicine did the job for my husband. it helped clear up his congestion but made him feel a bit sluggish during the day. still, I’d say it’s worth keeping in the medicine cabinet for when the season hits!

definitely a good tool to have at home. I used it for a family gathering to ease everyone’s minds, but it took longer to get results than I expected—about 20 minutes. still, it worked well and gives me some peace of mind, just wish it was quicker.

the kit works really well and is straightforward. I love the instructions were easy to follow, but I encountered a hiccup with the testing device itself that was slightly damaged when it arrived. contacted support, and they sorted it out, thankfully.

was skeptical about testing at home, but this kit changed my mind. easy and fast! took about 10 minutes for results, and it felt reliable. just wish the price was lower—$25 is a bit much for me to keep on hand constantly.

I appreciate how quickly I got results with this screening kit! under 10 minutes is a plus for someone busy. only downside was the somewhat less-than-clear instructions; I had to look up a video which was annoying.

easy to use, great instructions, and fast results make this screening test a hit in my house! I had a good experience with it, but I wish the test strips were a bit sturdier. one of mine bent during usage, which was annoying.

I was really impressed with this diagnostic kit. it felt professional, and the results correlated well with what my doctor said later. still, I was a little put off by how long it took to arrive—over a week. I would prefer a quicker delivery next time.

these rapid tests are a great tool to keep on hand. results are ready in no time! the only thing that bothered me was how the tests can feel a bit flimsy. had one break on me, which was disappointing. solid product overall, though!

I really dig how straightforward this kit is! got results in 10 minutes while waiting for dinner. my only complaint is that the packaging was a bit too complicated to open—had to use scissors. otherwise, great product!

this diagnostic kit was a solid choice for testing at home. I like that it had everything I needed included, but I did find the instructions could've been clearer. it took me a bit to wrap my head around how to use it fully. still, it performed well!

I grabbed this set for my twin boys, ages 7, and they can't get enough of it! they enjoy the freedom to create whatever comes to mind. the variety of shapes is fantastic! however, I’d recommend keeping an eye on the really tiny pieces because they tend to disappear quickly.

this building set is good but not great. my cousin (age 6) loves creating different structures with it. the quality is decent, but some pieces feel a bit flimsy. it serves as a great introduction to construction. a few more complex pieces would be nice to challenge him more.

my 5-year-old daughter enjoys making little houses and castles with this kit. I appreciate that it encourages her creativity! but, I wish there were more girl-themed pieces; all the figures are pretty generic. still, she plays with it every week and that’s what matters!

we purchased this modular set two months ago, and it's been a lifesaver during rainy days! my 8-year-old has built everything from cars to robots. the instructions are clear, yet some designs could use a few more detailed steps. overall, a good investment for imaginative play!

the building bricks in this kit are colorful and easy to snap together. my 3-year-old can grasp them well, which is a plus. however, I did find some pieces have sharp edges. just be cautious about that. he has his favorites and plays for hours, so that’s a win!

this set is fun but not perfect. my son (age 9) loves the idea of modular construction but some pieces tend to pop off easily. the range of pieces is impressive, yet I wish there were more instructions for advanced builds. still, he's happy and always ready to build again.

I love this modular building set! my daughter is 5 and she made a zoo with it - how cool is that? the colors are vibrant and it keeps her busy for hours. only thing is, some of the connection points could be stronger. but it's a great way for her to express creativity.

this construction kit is decent. my son, who’s 6, enjoys building cars with it. I like that it comes with small gears for movement, but we spent quite a bit of time searching for the right pieces since they’re not very organized in the box. a bit of a headache, but fun overall.

I bought this modular set for my niece (age 4) and it sparks her creativity! she loves building crazy structures. however, I noticed that some pieces don’t connect very well. it’s frustrating at times, but she’s thrilled to create her own designs. a solid choice for her age.

we got this building kit a month ago and it’s been a blast for my 7-year-old! he enjoys the challenge of building different models. the instructions are straightforward, although some parts are a bit flimsy. he still manages to create great stuff, just need to handle it with care.

this building set is awesome! my 8-year-old has created everything from robots to vehicles and loves how it can all connect in different ways. the only downside is that the instructions can sometimes be misleading, leaving him puzzled. still, it keeps him entertained for hours!

my daughter received this building set as a gift and she loves it! she's 6 and enjoys making all sorts of things. the modular design is great for her creativity, but some pieces are tricky for her little hands to maneuver. she still manages, and I help when needed.

I had high hopes for this building set but was slightly disappointed. my son (age 5) finds it fun, but the instructions can be confusing for him. I think it’s perfect for older kids, though. it’s colorful and offers lots of options, just not the best fit for younger kids.

this construction kit is pretty cool! my 6-year-old has built several structures already. I love that it encourages problem-solving skills, but honestly, some pieces are a bit hard to snap together. she still has a great time playing with it, so I guess it balances out.

my 9-year-old has had this set for a few months, and he's really enjoyed creating unique designs. the modular aspect is a big plus, allowing him to be super creative. however, some of the pieces are a bit fragile and have broken. still, he keeps coming back for more building!

this set was a great purchase for my daughter! she loves building houses with the modular pieces. the only issue we’ve faced is that some bricks don’t fit together as tightly as we would like. overall, she’s entertained, and that’s what matters most to me!

we bought this building set for our 7-year-old son, and he loves it! it has some really unique pieces that other sets don’t. he made a roller coaster track with it! however, some parts can be challenging to fit together. still, he gets creative and finds a way. it's fun to watch!

I got this kit for my son’s 5th birthday, and he was thrilled! the variety of modular pieces is fantastic, but I do wish the instructions were clearer. a few times we ended up with extra pieces and had to figure out where they went. overall, he enjoys it!

this building set is terrific for my 8-year-old! he’s been making all kinds of amazing structures. I love how the modular design allows him to be creative, but there are a couple of pieces that keep falling apart. he still enjoys it a lot overall!

my daughter recently got this construction kit and is obsessed! she’s 6 and has made so many different designs, but a few of the smaller pieces were tricky for her to handle. it’s fun to see her imagination at work, though! just keep an eye on those tiny pieces.

I appreciate the concept of this modular building set, but it leaves a bit to be desired. my 4-year-old enjoys it but sometimes struggles to assemble the pieces. they're colorful and engaging, yet finding the right pieces can be a hassle. still, she plays with it often.

these condoms are surprisingly thin! i was worried about them breaking, but they've held up well. got a box of 36 for around $20. the only downside is they can be a bit slippery, so just make sure you have a good lubricant on hand.

tried these birth control pills for the first time a few months ago. they work great in preventing pregnancy, but i have noticed some mood swings. not fun. overall, decent option, but make sure to talk to your doctor about side effects.

had the copper IUD for 6 months. insertion was painful for me, but after that, no issues! my periods are a bit heavier now, though. i like that it's hormone-free, but sometimes wish it didn’t cause these cramps. definitely not for everyone.

condoms are a must-have for me. I’ve tried a few brands, but these are really stretchy and fit well without the weird smell some others have. 12 for $15 seemed a bit pricey, but they feel worth it. just wish the packaging was less noisy!

I went for the implant last year. it’s been great because I don't have to think about it, but the insertion process was a bit uncomfortable. now I’m seeing some weight gain. really wanted to avoid that. overall, it has its pros and cons.

just started using these contraceptive pills after my doctor recommended them. I like that they’re easy to take and I can set a reminder on my phone. but honestly? I feel a bit nauseous sometimes. hoping it gets better with time.

these condoms fit really well and don’t have that weird texture some others do. I love how reliable they are, but the box is hard to open quietly. nothing like awkward moments when you’re trying to be discreet!

tried the hormonal IUD and while it’s nice not to have to think about birth control daily, I had some spotting for a few months. it was annoying, but now my periods are lighter. just a mixed bag really!
my partner loves these condoms! they don’t feel too restrictive, and they’re super thin. just wish they had a larger size option. overall, they’re pretty decent and we’ve had no breakage.

took these birth control pills for about 4 months now. they do prevent pregnancy, but man, the headaches! if you’re sensitive to hormonal changes, you might want to consider your options before diving in.

had the implant for two years, and it's been mostly great. no periods, yay! although I did have some bad acne. just something to be aware of, but I’d say it’s still a solid choice for long-term birth control.
I found these condoms really reliable, but my partner mentioned they felt a bit thicker than others we’ve used. we’re still happy with them, just not as ultra-sensitive as the package claims.

I started the pill about six weeks ago. they’re easy to take, but I’ve been feeling more emotional than usual. talk to your doctor if you have similar concerns, it’s important to find what works for you!

love the IUD for its longevity! it's been over a year now. only annoying part? cramps are sometimes worse than before. definitely discuss with your doctor if you're considering it. overall, I think I’d choose it again!

these condoms are okay. they fit well but seemed to lack some lubrication. I ended up using additional lube, which is annoying. not a total waste of money, but I might look for something else next time.

just got off the birth control implant after 3 years and let me tell you, I’m still figuring out my cycle! it does its job, but the hormonal changes were intense. would be careful if you have hormonal issues!
these condoms are pretty good but the box design makes it hard to open quietly. had a couple of 'oops' moments. other than that, they feel comfortable and secure.

I went with the IUD and honestly, I had mixed feelings. insertion was rough, but after, I've appreciated the hassle-free aspect. yet, my cramping has been quite annoying. make sure you're ready for it.
these contraceptive pills have definitely regulated my cycle, which is nice. but I have noticed some occasional nausea. not fun! still, they do what they promise, so overall not too bad.
I really like these condoms! they’re super comfortable and we didn’t have any issues with breakage. just wish they had a better variety in sizes. will continue to buy!

the birth control pills are working for me! I had a little spotting initially, but that went away. I appreciate that they help with my endometriosis, but feeling a bit tired lately. it’s a mixed bag for sure.
so I got the IUD about 5 months ago. it was pretty painful to get inserted but since then, I have not had to think about it, which I love! however, my periods are a bit unpredictable now.
these condoms are decent. I like that they come in a bulk package for a good price. however, they can sometimes break, which is scary. not sure if I’ll repurchase or try something else.
just had the implant put in a few weeks ago and I’m a bit anxious about gaining weight. I've heard mixed reviews about it. hope it works out because I love the idea of long-term birth control.
these condoms have been pretty reliable for us. no issues with breakage! the only thing I dislike is the packaging. it’s kind of loud and awkward to open when you want to maintain some privacy!
started the pill last month and they work well for preventing pregnancy. but my skin has been breaking out more than usual. really wish I could find one that doesn’t mess with my skin!
the IUD has been mostly good for me. it does take some getting used to, though! I wish I had more information on how to manage the side effects beforehand. ups and downs for sure!
these condoms have a nice classic feel to them. they fit snugly without feeling restrictive. I’d say they’re a good value for the money, but just be careful about the lubrication level!
I’ve been on these birth control pills for 3 months now and they seem to be doing their job. I have had some weird cravings, though! just balancing the pros and cons as I go.

the IUD process was pretty painful, but it’s nice not having to think about birth control daily! my periods are lighter now, which is great, but the cramps can be a bit intense. overall, mixed feelings!

this permanent hair dye by L'Oreal really surprised me! the color lasted about 6 weeks, which is longer than I expected for the price. the application was super easy, but I wish the smell was less strong. overall, good value for the money!

I tried the semi-permanent color from eSalon. it looked vibrant at first but faded quicker than I thought, about 3 weeks in, it started to wash out. love the color, just wish it lasted longer! my roots were showing way too soon.

the Garnier temporary hair color was a fun change for my daughter. she loved being a mermaid for her birthday! it washed out completely after just 2 washes, which was perfect for her. but I'd advise doing it in the shower because it stained my sink a bit.

used this hair color remover by Color Oops and it worked surprisingly well! it took my dark brown hair back to a light brown, but it left it a bit dry. make sure to condition well after. not bad for $15!

absolutely loved the vibrant red of this semi-permanent dye from Manic Panic! it was easy to apply and didn't smell bad. downside? it only lasted about 2 weeks, but I was cool with that because I wanted to try different colors anyway.

I have mixed feelings about this Clairol permanent dye. it covered my grays really well and the color was nice, but I did notice a bit of damage after. maybe I’ll switch to a better conditioner next time.

bought this temporary hair color in pink for my 7-year-old's party, and she was ecstatic! the color was vibrant, but it took several washes to come out completely. just be mindful, it may stain clothes if not careful!

this permanent dye from Revlon worked great for my friend's wedding! it covered my greys perfectly and lasted for about 8 weeks. still, I think the application could have been smoother. a bit messy in the bathroom!
I used the Color Charm semi-permanent dye for a fun weekend look. I loved how it felt on my hair, but it didn’t last as long as I had hoped. about 4 washes and it was pretty much gone.

would not recommend the temporary spray dye! I used it for a Halloween costume, and while it was fun for the night, it stained my hair and took days to wash out completely. definitely not what I was looking for!

tried the semi-permanent color from Schwarzkopf and I'm impressed! the shade was true to the box and it lasted about a month. however, it did fade slightly unevenly. also, the gloves they included were terrible, they ripped right away!

I bought this hair color remover from Ion and it worked wonders! I was really worried about my dark color, but it lifted it almost completely. I did have to use a deep conditioner after, though, because it made my hair feel brittle.
the Loreal Feria hair dye was okay. I loved the color payoff but honestly, it faded quicker than expected. about 3 weeks in, I was back to my roots. gonna need to touch it up sooner than I thought!

tried the Clairol semi-permanent hair color and was pleasantly surprised! it gave me a nice natural look for my grays, but I would say it only lasted about 5 washes before it started fading. still, easy to apply!

this temporary hair color from Splat is super fun! my friends and I had a blast dyeing our hair for a concert. it washes out easily, but it did leave some streaks on my hands. definitely a playful product!

I used this semi-permanent dye by Adore and was shocked how vibrant it was! it really clung to my hair and lasted about 6 weeks. the only thing was, it did dry my hair out a bit, so be prepared for that.

the hair color remover from Color Oops has its pros and cons. it worked well removing my dark dye but left my hair feeling a bit lifeless. I’d suggest following up with a good conditioning treatment afterward.

just tried the Garnier permanent hair color and I'm mixed about it. the color was beautiful and vibrant, but it did take a lot longer to set than the box suggested. still, I got compliments for weeks!
I wanted to love this semi-permanent dye from Crazy Color! the shade was amazing, but it washed out within 5 washes. great for a temporary change, but not ideal if you want something long-lasting.
bought this hair color remover and it did the job! i managed to go from dark brown to a lighter shade, but the smell was pretty strong. I recommend doing it in a well-ventilated area.

the Revlon ColorSilk permanent dye is a classic! I've used it for over 3 years and it never disappoints. great gray coverage and very affordable. however, the application can be a bit messy, so just be careful!
the color from this temporary dye was bright but faded super fast. I was hoping for it to last at least a week, but after 3 washes it was gone. fun for a one-time event, but not a long-term solution.

I used the Ion semi-permanent dye and it gave me the best results! my hair felt soft and the color was really rich. the only downside was that it did take a while to rinse out. but I'm happy with it overall!
this permanent hair dye by Clairol was good but not great. it covered most of my grays, but I expected the color to be more vibrant. it lasted about 4 weeks before I needed to reapply.
I tried the Splat semi-permanent dye for a festival and it was a total hit! the color was vibrant and fun. it did stain my hands a bit, which was annoying, but I loved how it looked.
the Color Oops remover worked pretty well for me. it lifted my dark dye, but I found it did leave a weird after smell. still, it was nice to switch up my color without too much damage.

I’ve noticed my ears feel much cleaner since I switched. However, I wish they were a bit longer for reaching deeper areas. Overall, happy with the purchase!

I got these cotton swabs as part of my first aid kit. They’re perfect for applying ointments or cleaning minor wounds. I like that they’re individually wrapped, so they're super hygienic. That said, they do feel a little flimsy compared to other brands, so I’m careful when using them. 4 stars!

I have a 3-year-old who always gets messy after arts and crafts. These cotton swabs are perfect for quick clean-ups! They’re soft enough for her sensitive skin and don’t leave any lint behind. My only complaint is that sometimes the tips start unraveling if I apply too much pressure. Still, I keep buying them!

Honestly, I'm a bit torn about these cotton swabs. They work excellently for cleaning but I expected them to be more absorbent. For the price, I thought they’d hold up better when cleaning up spills. They get the job done but just barely. Could be better!

I use them for everything from makeup application to cleaning small parts in my electronics. However, they do have a slight plastic smell upon opening which isn’t pleasant. Overall, good value!

I use them for spot treatments and love that they’re biodegradable. Just wish they were cheaper, but the eco-friendliness makes up for it somewhat.

For someone with sensitive ears, these cotton swabs are a lifesaver! I can clean my ears comfortably without any pain. My only small complaint is that they could be a little longer for better reach. But honestly, I’m very satisfied with how they perform!
