i've been using this calcium supplement for about three months now, and i do feel stronger. but the pills are HUGE! sometimes, they get stuck in my throat, which is annoying. might look for something smaller next time.
i tried the fertility tea blend and honestly, i'm not sure yet if it’s working. i like the taste, but it feels like a long shot. i’ll give it a couple more months before deciding. fingers crossed!
the reusable heating pad is fantastic! i use it for my cramps and it's so much better than the old hot water bottle. just wish the battery lasted longer; it tends to die after about 2 hours.

these menstrual cups are a game-changer for me! way less waste than pads and tampons, and more comfortable once you get the hang of it. my only issue was the first couple of tries were a bit messy, but now i'm a pro!
had high hopes for this menopause cream, but it didn’t help with my night sweats at all. it feels nice on my skin, but that’s about it. i think i’ll stick to my regular routine instead.
these omega-3 gummies are so tasty, but they got a little sticky in the bottle. i have to pry them out sometimes. overall, though, my skin looks better, and i enjoy taking them daily!
the prenatal DHA supplement is a must-have for my pregnancy. i noticed it helps with my brain fog. only downside is they’re a bit large, which makes it hard to take with my morning nausea.

this magnesium supplement really calmed my cycle symptoms, but it took a while to notice the effects. about three weeks in, and finally feeling a difference! also, the price isn't bad at all compared to other brands.
the menopause support tea is comforting, especially at night. it’s soothing, but sometimes the flavor can be a bit too floral for my taste. still, it’s a nice little routine to wind down with.
the fertility supplement sets are easy to take but definitely on the pricey side. i've been using them for a month, and I guess we'll see if they make a difference. i’m hopeful but cautious!

these ovulation strips are pretty straightforward to use and help me identify my fertile window. however, they can be a bit confusing with the lines sometimes. just take your time figuring it all out!
the reusable menstrual pads are a great eco-friendly option. they’re super soft and absorbent. my only issue is remembering to wash them; I've had a couple of mishaps with stains.
the women's probiotic really improved my gut health, but the pills are huge! took a while to get used to swallowing them. still, the benefits are worth the struggle. i’ll keep taking them!
i bought this herbal menopause support because of the good reviews. it did help a little with my anxiety, but didn’t do much for the hot flashes. i might try a different one next time.
the menstrual cramp relief oil smells amazing and works well for relaxation, but the rollerball applicator can be a bit messy. i often find it drips down my wrist!
these dietary supplements for postpartum health are packed with nutrients, but they gave me a little stomach upset at first. after a week, my body adjusted and now i feel great!
this cycle tracking journal is such a cute way to keep notes, but i wish it had more space for daily symptom tracking. still, love writing down my thoughts and feelings about my health journey!
the herbal tincture for PMS helps me chill, but the taste is pretty intense. it works, but it’s a hard sell to get down on bad days! still, it beats feeling awful.
i really like this multi-vitamin for women, but it has a strong smell that isn’t pleasant. on the upside, it’s helping with my energy levels, so i might overlook the smell for that boost!
the warm compress pads are a lifesaver during my period! heat feels so good. unfortunately, they don't stick that well and tend to slip around if you're moving too much. still, they help ease pain.
the evening primrose oil capsules have been nice for my skin, but I haven’t noticed much change in my cycle. easy to take, though! just wish they did a bit more for my period symptoms.

this changing table pad cover is super soft! i love how plush it feels. it's waterproof, so spills are no big deal. however, it took a while to air out the smell when i first got it. after a week, though, it was perfect! fits my standard pad well too.

i got this cover about 2 months ago, and it’s been great for my little one. machine washable is a must for me, and it holds up nicely after multiple washes. my only complaint is that sometimes it shifts around a bit on the pad.

bought this for my daughter’s changing table. it’s gorgeous, the color is vibrant! but honestly, i expected it to be a bit thicker. definitely not as plush as i thought it would be, but it does the job and cleans easily.

ok, so this cover has a really nice feel, but it has attracted some lint after a few washes. i wish it were a bit more durable. love the waterproof feature though; it saved me a lot of clean-up on those diaper disasters!

we’ve had this for about 4 months now and it’s really held up. super easy to clean, just toss it in the wash! however, it can be a little slippery sometimes, which worries me a bit when changing my squirmy baby.

got this to replace an older cover, and I’m really happy! it feels plush and comfy for my babe. i do wish the colors were a bit more varied, but the quality for the price is solid. just good for a changing pad!
this cover has been great! super soft and my baby loves it. however, it didn't fit my changing pad as snug as I'd hoped. it tends to bunch up a little if my little one moves around too much.

my son is 1 and he gets a bit wild during changes. this cover has been amazing because it’s waterproof. one downside is that it required a bit of time to dry after washing, which isn't always ideal when you're in a hurry!

love the softness of this pad cover. very plush! i use it for my baby's changing table, and it’s super cozy. only downside is it seems to pick up pet hair like crazy. i have to lint-roll it every few days!

this has been a lifesaver during diaper changes! waterproof feature is awesome, but it did have a chemical smell when it first arrived. i washed it twice, and that helped a lot. now it's perfect for my 2-year-old.

the material is really good and feels nice, but mine came with a small tear. customer service was helpful and sent a replacement quickly. the new one is perfect, but I wish the quality control was better.
the cover fits well and looks cute! however, after a couple of washes, it lost some of its plush feel. still effective and easy to clean, but a little disappointed it didn’t stay as soft.
i've been using this for 6 months, and it’s held up well! it’s machine washable, which is such a lifesaver for those messy changes. just wish it came in more designs to match my nursery decor!
absolutely love this changing pad cover! it’s soft and super easy to clean. my baby seems comfortable on it, but it does slide around a bit. i just wish it had a better grip underneath.

after using this for a few weeks, I can say it’s pretty good. soft and plush, makes changing a bit nicer for baby. however, I find that it doesn’t always fit snuggly on our changing pad, which is a bummer.

got this cover because it was recommended, and it’s been great! fits well on a standard changing pad and is really easy to wash. but I feel like the plushness could be better. it feels a bit thin in spots.
i like the waterproof feature; it’s truly done wonders for keeping the changing pad clean. however, it tends to bunch up at the corners, which can be annoying. overall, it’s decent for the price!
my little one loves this changing pad cover! it’s so soft, perfect for when he wiggles during changes. but, I wish it had more of a non-slip backing, as it shifts too much for my liking sometimes.
i bought this about a month ago, and it’s been great for diaper duty! very soft and plush. however, it does take a while to dry after washing. would be nice if it dried faster!

the plush feel is wonderful on this cover! my baby stays comfortable during changes. the only downside is that it stains a bit easily. getting those diaper messes out can be tricky, even with washing.
this is a really nice changing pad cover! it fits well and is waterproof, which is crucial. my only real issue is that it can sometimes get a bit wrinkled after washing, but overall, I’m happy.
I’ve been using this for my twins, and it’s held up well so far. the plush material is great, but it slides a bit on the changing table. still, it’s a good deal for the price, so I’m satisfied.
the softness of this pad is fantastic! my baby seems comfortable on it, but it did have a weird smell out of the package. thankfully, it went away after a wash, now it’s perfect!
i really like this cover! it’s easy to clean and looks nice, but I found it picks up dirt more than I expected. for the price, still a good buy, just need to wash more often than I thought.
this cover is awesome for my baby! waterproof is a big win. it does seem to fade a bit after washing, which is somewhat disappointing. but for the comfort it provides, I can't complain too much!
just got this cover a week ago, and I’m impressed with how soft it is. my baby loves it! the machine washable feature is great, but it did take a few washes for it to lose the initial smell.
the cover is adorable and fits well! I love that it’s waterproof, especially with a messy toddler. however, the material isn’t as plush as I hoped, so it could be more comfortable.
my husband and I bought this for our changing table, and we both love how it looks. it’s so easy to clean, but I do wish there was more variety in colors! still a nice pad cover overall.

after struggling with allergies for years, my acupuncturist suggested treatments. it's been 4 months and i've seen a noticeable improvement. sessions can be a bit pricey, around $90 each, but spending less on allergy meds is worth it for me!

tried a naturopathy approach for my digestion issues. the herbal remedies worked somewhat, but it took about 6 weeks to feel any difference. i love the natural aspect of it but the wait was frustrating. just wish there was a quicker fix.

went to a chiropractor after my back started acting up because of my 2-year-old. the first adjustment felt amazing but honestly, after a couple of visits, i wasn't feeling much change anymore. really depends on the person, i guess.

the herbal tinctures from this naturopath include ingredients like ginger and turmeric, which i love. i noticed my inflammation decreasing, but the taste is really strong! not something i’d want daily, but it does help!

acupuncture is a bit intimidating, but my friend convinced me to try it. I've had 3 sessions so far, and I feel more relaxed than I have in ages. just wish they were easier to fit into my schedule; it’s hard to make time!

saw a chiropractor for my constant migraines, and after a few weeks, I finally found some relief. however, the visits are costly, averaging $75 each, and my insurance barely covers it. a mixed bag overall.

I've been using herbal remedies for my anxiety, and I really like the calming effect of the valerian root. it took about a week to kick in, but the taste of the drops is a bit off-putting. not sure if I’d stick to it long-term.

gave acupuncture a shot for my chronic pain, and wow, I felt relief after just one session! it was about $85, which isn't cheap, but worth every penny if this keeps up! just not a fan of needles, but I’m pushing through for the pain relief!

chiropractic care has helped with my lower back pain. I get adjusted every 2 weeks, and it's made a noticeable difference. still, I find myself needing to go back to keep the pain at bay, so I don't know how long this will last.

herbal teas have been a nice addition to my wellness routine. I love chamomile and peppermint for relaxation, but I wish there were more convenient single-serve options. brewing takes a bit of time, but worth it for the soothing experience.

naturopathy was recommended by a friend, and I started seeing someone local for my anxiety. I'm about 2 months in with herbal supplements. they help, but I wish we could communicate better about the doses. some days, I’m unsure if I’m doing it right.

I’ve had mixed feelings about acupuncture. my first session was amazing, but my last 3 haven’t felt as effective. maybe I need a different acupuncturist? the experience is relaxing, but I want to feel those results consistently!

saw a lot of reviews about this chiropractor, so gave it a shot after my back went out. first visit felt great, but the package deal ($300 for 5 visits) felt a bit steep. I guess I’ll see if it actually helps long-term.

I’ve combined acupuncture with yoga for my chronic pain, and they complement each other well. it’s been a slow journey, but I feel more balanced. the downside is the time commitment; scheduling everything is a challenge with my busy life.

tried a few herbal supplements for seasonal allergies, and they did help reduce my symptoms! but honestly, the medication was easier because I didn’t have to remember to take it three times a day. that said, I'm loving the natural route.

my chiropractor offers a wellness plan that sounded good but turned out to be too inflexible for my lifestyle. my back pain improved but I found it hard to commit to all the appointments. just wish it was more adaptable to my schedule.

started seeing an acupuncturist for stress relief, and after 5 sessions, I’ve noticed I handle stress better. she's great at explaining the process, but honestly, the needles can be scary! still, I’d recommend trying it at least once.

the herbal remedy I tried for headaches was clove oil, and while it worked most times, the application process can be messy. I like that it’s natural, but it took a bit to find the right amount to use without overwhelming the pain.

chiropractic care worked wonders for my neck pain, but I’m worried about the long-term costs. I pay about $60 per visit, and it adds up quickly when I’m told I should see them weekly. still, I love how it feels afterward!

I've been doing herbs for skin issues and they seem to improve my complexion a bit, but the changes are subtle. it’s nice that they’re organic, but I wish I saw results faster. patience is key, I guess!

started acupuncture for stress management and the relaxation is nice! but it’s hard to justify spending $100 every session. would love to see some more affordable options. it's great for temporary relief, just can’t keep it up.

my experience with chiropractic care has been decent. the adjustments help, but sometimes I leave feeling sore. it's like a mixed bag, and figuring out the best frequency has been a challenge. just happy when I feel relief!

the herbal creams for joint pain are interesting. they help a little, but the consistency is strange, and I find myself reapplying often. it’s better than taking pills, though, but I wish it lasted longer!

gave acupuncture a try for sleep issues. the first few treatments were surprisingly calming, but I still struggle to stay asleep through the night. I’m thinking about sticking with it a bit longer to really see if it helps.

my first chiropractic session left me feeling great, but the follow-up visits didn't have the same effect. I loved the initial relief, but I have mixed feelings about the repetitive nature of going back. might try something else soon.

herbal sleep aids have been a lifesaver! I tried valerian root and it helped me sleep better, though I still wake up occasionally. the taste isn’t my fave, but at least I’m not tossing and turning for hours anymore!
went for some naturopath advice regarding my fatigue. got a plan with dietary changes and some herbal supplements, but it seems overwhelming. it’s been a month, and I’m unsure about the results yet!

tried a few chiropractic visits for my shoulder pain but can’t seem to find the right adjustment. some days I feel better, while other days it’s like nothing changes. I’m a bit frustrated, and it feels hit or miss!

I’ve been experimenting with acupuncture for my migraines, and it can be soothing, but I’m unsure if it’s the entire solution. feeling a bit better, but still need to figure out my triggers while I keep this up.

my experience with herbal remedies for anxiety was positive! I tried a blend with passionflower, which seemed to help a lot, but the taste was tough to get down. I’m glad it’s natural, but not the best for my palate!

chiropractic care was helpful for my hip pain initially, but the relief didn't last as long as I'd hoped. the adjustments felt good, but I might have to try a different approach to see if I can find a better fix.

this step stool is perfect for my 3-year-old. the adjustable height feature is a lifesaver since we just transitioned from diapers. it’s sturdy and has a nonslip surface, which gives me peace of mind. however, it’s a bit bulkier than I expected, making it harder to tuck away when not in use.

just got this for my 2-year-old who's potty training. the foldable design is great because it saves space in our tiny bathroom. i love the weight capacity too; it feels really sturdy. however, it did take a bit of time to figure out the height adjustment. overall, it’s a solid step stool!

after looking for a safe step stool, i found this one. it's got a nonslip surface that actually works! my kid hasn't slipped once, which is a big deal. the only downside is that it feels a bit heavy to carry around. wish it was lighter since we move it between the bathroom and kitchen.

my toddler is obsessed with this step stool! the adjustable height fit perfectly with our new toilet. however, the foldable design could be better; it can be a little tricky to fold back up. still, it feels very sturdy and has a good weight capacity, so that’s a plus.

i was excited to get this step stool after hearing great things. it’s definitely sturdy and has a nonslip surface, but i wish the height adjustment was easier. it can be a little cumbersome, especially when holding my little one. it's nice overall, but could improve on ease of use.

for about $30, this step stool is a good buy. the nonslip surface is excellent for my clumsy 3-year-old, and the adjustable height means we won’t have to buy a new one anytime soon. that said, it does take some effort to fold, which can be annoying when you’re in a rush.

this stool has been lifesaving during potty training. it’s adjustable, which is awesome because i can set it high or low as he grows. sometimes it’s a bit wobbly though, so i’m always by his side when he’s using it. overall, a pretty good buy for the price.

i love how lightweight this step stool is! my 4-year-old can move it around easily, and the foldable design is a huge bonus for storage. however, the nonslip surface could be stickier; it sometimes slides a bit on our tile floor. still, it's working for us!

we bought this step stool for our 2-year-old, and she absolutely loves it! it’s also got a good weight capacity, which is great as I can stand on it too. the only downside is it can be tough to adjust the height quickly. other than that, it’s a solid product!

the foldable design of this step stool is fantastic! it fits right in our closet. my daughter loves to use it for reaching the sink. however, the surface could be a bit wider; it feels a bit cramped sometimes. overall, it’s a decent stool for potty training.

got this for my son when we started potty training, and I’m impressed. the nonslip surface is fantastic, but the height adjustment mechanism can be a bit tricky to operate. sometimes I need two hands to get it right. still, it’s helping him climb up safely.

this step stool is alright. the adjustable height is a huge perk for our growing toddler, but it feels a little heavy for me to lift around. if it were a bit lighter and easier to fold, I’d rate it higher. nonetheless, it serves its purpose.

i was looking for a safe option for my now-3-year-old. the height features are great, but honestly, the nonslip surface could be improved. we've had a few slips, which makes me cautious. it’s decent overall, but I wish it had a better grip.

just got this for my 3-year-old, and it's pretty cool! the foldable design is handy, but I wish it were foldable with one hand. my daughter likes the height adjustment, but it can be a bit stiff. overall, a good stool for the price point!

this stool has really helped my 2.5-year-old get used to using the toilet. the weight capacity is impressive, but I do wish it had a slightly more secure grip on the floor. it sometimes shifts a bit when my kid climbs up. still, a decent investment!

the adjustable height on this step stool is genius. it really makes a difference when trying to reach the toilet. however, the nonslip surface isn’t as effective on our wooden floor, which makes me slightly nervous. I’m thinking of adding some rubber pads to help out.

bought this stool for my potty training toddler. love the foldable design, but I must say, it can be tough to adjust the height quickly. it feels sturdy, but I do wish the surface was wider for added safety. all in all, it’s been worth the money.

my child loves the ability to adjust this step stool! it’s a little heavy but feels well made. the nonslip surface is definitely a plus. however, it’s a bit awkward to fold back down when you’re in a hurry. still, it's been helpful for potty time.

this step stool is exactly what we needed! the adjustable height lets my kids use it comfortably, and the nonslip features really work. my only complaint is that it can be bulky to store. if it were a bit more compact, it would be perfect!

I purchased this for my 3-year-old for potty training, and it’s been really great. I appreciate the solid construction, and the nonslip surface gives me peace of mind. that said, the height adjustment isn’t super intuitive and takes some practice. overall, happy with it!

this step stool is pretty good for my toddler. the adjustable height is great, and it feels safe enough, but the foldable design could be easier to manage. sometimes it gets stuck when I try to fold it up. other than that, it’s working well for potty training.

the weight capacity is impressive for this step stool. I can even stand on it to help my daughter reach the sink. however, I did find the nonslip surface could be better; it slides a bit on our tile. still, it's been great for potty training!

just what we needed during potty training! i love the foldable feature; it makes it easy to tuck away when not in use. my only issue is the adjustable height can be tricky. still a solid step stool that feels safe for my child.

this stool has been working well for potty training. the adjustable height is a huge plus, but the nonslip surface isn’t as good as I expected; my child slipped once. that said, it’s sturdy, and I feel comfortable letting my kid use it.

this step stool has a few things I love—the nonslip surface is awesome for safety, and it's adjustable! but the foldable design isn’t super easy and can be cumbersome. overall decent, but some improvements could make it perfect.

this stroller is a lifesaver! the folding mechanism is super easy to use, and i can do it one-handed, which is great when my 2-year-old is having a meltdown. love the adjustable handlebars, too, since my partner and i are different heights. but the canopy could be a bit bigger for those sunny days!

the suspension system makes for a smooth ride on all terrains! we’ve taken it on gravel and grass without any issues. however, the cup holder is a little flimsy, and i wish it could hold a larger coffee cup without spilling.

overall, a solid stroller. i appreciate how the seat reclines deeply for nap time. my baby loves the five-point harness, but the weight of the stroller is something to consider—it can get heavy when loaded with groceries too! still, it’s manageable.

the swivel wheels are a nice touch, especially in crowded places. i can maneuver this stroller with ease. but the removable canopy is a bit tricky to get back on after washing. still, it looks great and works well after 6 months of use!

i had high hopes for this stroller, and it mostly delivers. the all-terrain tires are fantastic, and the ride is super smooth for my little one. but i do find the folding mechanism is not as smooth as advertised, sometimes requiring two tries.

this is my go-to stroller! i love how lightweight it is but still very sturdy. the five-point harness keeps my rambunctious toddler secure. my only complaint is the size when folded; it takes up a good amount of trunk space.

the adjustable handlebars are awesome for tall parents like me, and the reclining seat is perfect for long walks. however, the cup holder is a little too small to fit my travel mug, so i wish that was better designed!

i've had this stroller for about 4 months now, and it works great! the suspension system really does help with bumps in the road. one downside is that the storage basket isn't as big as I hoped, making it tough to carry everything we need.

the folding mechanism is nice but not as compact as I expected. it takes a bit of getting used to. the swivel wheels, though, are amazing for turning corners! a solid buy if you prioritize maneuverability.

really happy with this stroller! the removable canopy is a breeze to clean, and it has held up well after multiple washes. my child seems super comfortable in the reclining seat. i just wish the cup holder was attached more securely.

it’s good overall, but the weight of the stroller can be a struggle when lifting it into the car. on the bright side, the all-terrain tires are a dream for walks in the park! i’d still recommend it for the price.
i love how easy it is to push! the adjustable handlebars are such a plus when my partner takes a turn. the only downside is that it doesn't fit well in my smaller hatchback—something to keep in mind!

the five-point harness is very secure, which gives me peace of mind while we’re out and about. the reclining seat is perfect for when my baby falls asleep. just wish the cup holder was bigger, as I often spill my coffee!

i bought this stroller after researching for weeks. the suspension system is fantastic for outdoor walks, but the folding process can be a hassle sometimes. still, my 18-month-old loves it, and that's what matters most!

this stroller has a great design and feels sturdy! the adjustable handlebars are a huge win for my tall husband. however, the storage basket is smaller than expected, which is a bit of a letdown when we go out for the day.

it works well over rough terrain, and the one-hand fold is convenient. however, it’s a bit bulky when folded, which makes it hard to fit in the trunk sometimes. the swivel wheels are a lifesaver for maneuvering through tight spaces.

i bought this for my 3-month-old, and it has been excellent so far! the reclining feature is handy for nap time. however, the cup holder doesn’t fit standard size drinks, which is a bit annoying during long trips.

the all-terrain tires really do make a difference when we’re on the go. love that it has a removable canopy for those sunny days! the only downside is that the stroller can feel a bit heavy when pushing uphill.
i’ve used it for about 5 months now. it’s easy to fold, and the adjustable handlebars are great for me and my partner. my only gripe is that the fabric attracts dirt, and I have to clean it often.

this stroller has some great features! the reclining seat is fantastic for my little one and he seems so comfortable. I wish the cup holder was a bit sturdier, though; it feels like it could break easily.

amazing stroller! the suspension system works wonders on bumpy streets. i also appreciate the five-point harness for extra safety. my only complaint is the weight—definitely feels heavier than other strollers.

love the smooth movement of the swivel wheels. it feels so easy to control! but the storage basket isn’t the biggest, so I often have to carry a tote bag with us. overall, a solid purchase for city living.

very satisfied with this purchase! the adjustable handlebars are a great feature, and my baby seems super comfy reclined. one downside is that the cup holder isn’t very stable; my drink fell once when I hit a bump.

i was looking for something lightweight but sturdy, and this fits the bill! the folding mechanism is easy, but sometimes it won't fold completely on the first try. still, it’s a good investment for our family outings.

the removable canopy is a lifesaver in the summer! it’s easy to detach when I want to clean it. my only issue is the stroller feels a bit cumbersome at times, especially navigating through tight stores.

the beard oil from this line is phenomenal! i’ve tried a few others, but this one really softens my scruff and gives it a nice shine. it has a subtle woodsy scent that’s not overpowering. only downside: the dropper can be a bit messy sometimes. overall, pretty happy with it!

the shaving cream is decent. it lathers up pretty well and does give a smooth shave, but i found that it leaves my skin a bit dry afterward. it’s nice that it has natural ingredients, but i wish they’d include a moisturizing element. might not buy again.

picked up the men's grooming kit last month. it came with a trimmer, scissors, and comb. the trimmer is really powerful and gives a clean cut, but the comb feels a bit flimsy. for about $40, it’s a solid deal for starting out with grooming.

i’m loving the aftershave balm! it’s super soothing and doesn’t sting like some others. my skin usually gets irritated after shaving, but this has helped a lot. only wish it came in a bigger bottle because i might run out too soon!

the face moisturizer is nice, absorbs quickly. however, the scent is a bit strong for my taste. it does keep my skin hydrated all day, but I just wish the fragrance was more subtle. still, it has lasted me about three months, so not bad overall.

this beard wash was a game changer for me. it doesn’t strip the natural oils from my beard and leaves it feeling clean without being dry. the minty scent is refreshing, especially in the morning. but it could use a better pump, as it sometimes clogs.

got the beard conditioner recently, and let me tell you, my beard feels amazing! it's much softer than before, and the scent isn’t overpowering. just wish it came in a larger size because I’m going through it pretty fast. still, love this stuff!

the electric razor works well for a close shave but takes a while to charge. i had to wait almost 8 hours before i could use it the first time. it’s efficient, though, and has a nice grip. just a bit annoying with the charging time.

i bought the grooming scissors set a few weeks ago. they’re sharp and precise, perfect for trimming my mustache. however, they feel a little lightweight and could've been more sturdy. overall, they do the job but maybe not the best quality.

the beard grooming kit is fantastic! it comes with oil, balm, and a comb. my beard has never looked so neat and stylish. the only thing is the comb is kind of small, and I prefer a larger one. still, worth the price of about $25!

the skincare set is a mixed bag. the face wash is gentle, but I didn’t see any difference in my skin after three weeks. maybe it works for others, but it’s just okay for me. the moisturizer is great, though – very hydrating!

i really like the scent of the body spray, but it doesn’t last long at all. I sprayed it on this morning, and by noon, it was almost gone. pretty disappointing considering the price tag. might have to look for something stronger.

the hair styling cream does a solid job keeping my hair in place throughout the day. it’s not sticky, which I appreciate, but it can feel a bit heavy if you use too much. a little goes a long way, which is a plus!

bought the multi-use beard trimmer and it works pretty well. it does all the basic tasks, but the battery life isn’t too impressive – I have to charge it every week even with minimal use. decent for the price, but I expected better.

the cologne is nice and subtle, perfect for daily wear. just wish it had a bit more staying power. I find myself needing to reapply after a few hours. it's fresh, though, so I'll probably keep using it until I find something else.

i’m really impressed with the grooming products! the face scrub is super effective and leaves my skin feeling fresh. but, the scent is a bit too strong for my liking. overall, it’s a good value for the price, just wish the fragrance was milder.

the beard balm is something I didn’t know I needed! it tames my wild beard and adds a nice shine. the only problem is that it can get a bit greasy if you apply too much. I’ve learned to use just a small amount for best results.

the shaving brush is nice, but the bristles feel a bit too rough for my sensitive skin. it does create a good lather, but I wish it was softer. I might keep it around, but I'll look for a better one later on.

the travel grooming kit is super convenient! it has everything I need in a compact size. I took it on a trip recently and it fit perfectly in my bag. only thing I wish it had was a better quality razor – it’s okay, but not as sharp as I’d like.

the aftershave splash is refreshing, but it's a bit too alcohol-heavy for my taste. I like how it cools my skin down after shaving, but the burn is something I could do without. not sure I'd buy it again just for that reason.

the deodorant has a nice clean scent and lasts all day. I’ve used it for a month now without any issues. my only complaint is that it sometimes leaves white marks on my shirts. overall, it's been a solid choice for the price.

the beard growth oil seems to be working for me, though I’m not sure how quickly. I’ve been using it for about 2 weeks and have noticed some new growth. it smells lovely, but I wish the bottle was larger – it’s going fast!

the grooming wipes are a lifesaver! I keep them in my gym bag for freshening up after workouts. they’re not too strong in scent and remove sweat really well. my only issue is that the package can be hard to reseal after opening.
the face sunblock is awesome! it’s lightweight and doesn’t leave a greasy film on my skin. however, it is a bit pricey for a small tube. I’d use it daily if it was a bit more budget-friendly.

the nose hair trimmer works surprisingly well! it’s compact and gets the job done without pain. my only issue is finding the right angle – sometimes it takes a few tries to get everything. but overall, I’m quite satisfied with it.

the beard shaping tool is cool, but it took me a few tries to get used to it. once I figured it out, it helped me create some nice lines, but it could be a bit more intuitive. still, it’s a useful addition to my grooming routine.

the electric toothbrush is decent, but the battery life could be better. I have to charge it every couple weeks. it does clean my teeth well, so I’m happy with that. just wish it didn’t require so much upkeep.

the body wash smells great and lathers nicely, but I didn’t notice any moisturizing effect. my skin feels a little dry afterward. I might switch back to my old brand just for that reason, but the scent is definitely inviting.

these tide pods are super convenient! i love that there's no measuring involved, just pop one in and go. my clothes come out smelling fresh and clean. however, i wish they were a bit more effective on tough stains, like grass and red wine. overall, a solid product for everyday laundry.

downy fabric softener has been my go-to for years. it makes my clothes feel so soft and smell amazing! but i noticed it can sometimes leave a residue on dark fabrics if you use too much, so just be careful. other than that, it’s fantastic for keeping everything comfy.

finally got my hands on this eco-friendly laundry detergent, and it’s been a nice change! it’s gentle on my sensitive skin and works well, but i have to use a bit more than the recommended amount for really dirty loads. it's about $15 for a small bottle, so keep that in mind!

oxiclean stain remover is a lifesaver! my toddler gets so many stains on her clothes, and this stuff tackles them like a champ. the only downside is that the spray bottle can clog up sometimes. just a little annoying, but it gets the job done in the end.

arm & hammer laundry detergent is decent, especially for the price. i love that it has a nice, fresh scent and is great for regular washes, but it struggles a bit with stains. i usually end up pre-treating before washing, which adds an extra step i’d rather avoid.

been trying this method laundry detergent and it's so different from what i used before! way less chemicals, which is great, but the scent is pretty subtle, almost too subtle for my taste. i like my clothes to smell fresh. it’s just okay for me, but i appreciate the eco-friendly aspect.

decided to try out these purex pods, and they work pretty well! my clothes come out clean, but they don’t quite have the same fresh scent as other brands. if you're looking for something budget-friendly, this is a decent option, but don’t expect them to wow you.

i love this snuggle fabric softener! it leaves my linens feeling like a dream and smelling great. my only complaint is the bottle – sometimes it’s hard to pour without spilling everywhere. a pump would be way easier for this kind of product.

laundry is so much easier with these tide pods! they dissolve perfectly in cold water, which is a must for me. the only thing? they don’t always work on heavy stains. i usually have to treat those spots before throwing in a pod. still, i’d say they’re worth a try!

grabbed this seventh generation laundry detergent because I wanted something a bit more natural. it does a decent job, but i feel like my whites aren’t as bright as they used to be. if you're sensitive to scents, this is a good choice, though!

this all stainlifter detergent is perfect for my husband’s work clothes! they often get covered in grease and dirt, and this stuff really does a good job getting it out. just wish the scent was a little stronger; sometimes, I can't smell anything after washing.

the arm & hammer scent booster really works wonders! my clothes smell amazing after washing. however, it's easy to use too much, and it can leave a residue. great for towels and sheets, but I’m cautious using it on darker colors. all in all, a nice product!

i finally tried the woolite delicate laundry detergent for my sweaters and delicates. my items feel soft and look great after washing! downside? it’s kind of pricey for a small bottle. i'll keep using it but only for my favorite clothes, i guess.

tide's ultra stain release detergent has been my lifesaver with kids! it tackles everything from spaghetti to mud. on the flip side, it’s a bit expensive compared to other brands, but the results make it worth it for me.

the bounce dryer sheets are the best! they keep my clothes soft and static-free. my only issue is that I wish they had more scents available. sometimes, I just want a change from their classic fresh. overall, I can’t do laundry without them!

tried out the ecos laundry detergent, and I really appreciate the eco-friendly ingredients. but I’m not thrilled with how it performs on heavy stains. I think it’s fine for regular loads, but I might stick to a stronger detergent when I have tougher stuff.

fabric softener from snuggle really softens my blankets nicely! but I did notice it can cause a bit of a buildup in my washer, so I have to run a clean cycle now and then. still, the smell is lovely and really lasts.

got my hands on this arm & hammer detergent in a bulk pack, and I’m happy with how long it’s lasting! it cleans well and has a light scent. my only issue is that it can leave clothes feeling a tad stiff if I skip the softener.

i’m amazed at how well this zout stain remover works! spilled coffee on my favorite shirt, and it came out like magic. however, the smell is a bit overwhelming for me. if they could tone down the fragrance, I’d be all set!

the purex fabric softener is good, but I don’t love the scent. it gets the job done, making my clothes soft, but I prefer something with a fresher aroma. it’s also pretty affordable, so I might keep using it anyway because it works.

found this tide free & gentle detergent for my daughter's sensitive skin. it’s been great so far! no rashes or reactions. the only downside is that it doesn’t have much of a scent, but that’s okay for us. better safe than sorry, I suppose.

love these gain flings for laundry! they smell wonderful and make everything soft. just wish they were a bit cheaper since I go through them fast. but honestly, they’ve made laundry less of a chore to tackle lately!

the downy wrinkle releaser is a pretty cool product! I love that I can spray it on my work clothes right before I head out. but I’ve noticed it doesn’t completely remove stubborn wrinkles. it’s a nice quick fix, though, when I’m in a crunch!

was skeptical about this method stain remover at first, but it did a surprisingly good job on a berry stain! the only thing is that it took a bit of scrubbing. not huge, but if I’m in a hurry, that could be annoying. still, I’ll keep it handy!

tried the eco-friendly laundry pods and they’re pretty decent! I like that they’re better for the environment, but I wouldn’t say they work as well as traditional options. if you’re set on green products, give them a go, but be ready to pre-treat stains.

the snuggle plus super care is a nice upgrade from regular fabric softener! it keeps my clothes smelling fresh for days. I just wish it was available in larger bottles, as I’m always running out and need it for all my laundry!

the cascade platinum pods are not just for dishes, but I used them as laundry detergent on a whim, and it worked surprisingly well! just a little weird to use dish detergent for clothes, but if you’re in a pinch, this could do the trick!

these crib rail covers have been a lifesaver! my little one is teething and was gnawing on the wooden rails. now he can chew on these safely. they fit perfectly on my standard crib, but wish they had more color options. overall, good purchase!

got these crib rail guards and honestly, they do keep my baby from biting the wood. however, they slide down a bit and I have to adjust them every week. i love the soft material though - it feels safe for my 18-month-old.

we've been using these rail protectors for about a month now. they look nice and my toddler loves them. however, they’re not as thick as I expected, so I hope they hold up for a long time. for $25, they should last!

not bad for the price, but installation was a pain. i almost gave up! that said, they look cute on the crib and my son likes to play with them. they do the job, but be prepared to spend some time getting them on.

these crib teething guards came in handy! my 2-year-old was chewing the crib like it was a snack. now she's content with these covers, but one side keeps slipping off. it's annoying, but manageable. would buy again.

I was hesitant to buy these crib rail pads because of reviews saying they come off easily. I took a chance and so far, they’ve stayed put for 4 weeks. they’re adorable and do a good job protecting the rails but could use better adhesion.

love the design and softness of these rail covers! my daughter loves to cuddle with them. however, they don’t fully cover the ends of the rails, which makes me nervous sometimes. overall, good, but could be better!
these rail guards saved our crib from some major teeth marks. they fit snugly, but I wish the colors weren't so limited. my child loves them but they're a bit hard to clean. still, I’d say worth it.

the material on these crib rail covers is super soft, but they don't seem to be very durable. my 1-year-old is a strong chewer, and some of the fabric is already fraying after a month! they look nice, but I need something sturdier.
we’ve been using these for a few weeks now, and they’re doing fairly well. they attach easily, but I have to readjust them often. they protect the wood and look good, just wish the grip was stronger.

these crib teething guards are okay. I love the colors and they really brighten up the nursery! however, they don’t seem to fit securely, so I worry about my daughter pulling them off while trying to climb. mixed feelings!

I really like how soft these crib rail covers are! my baby is teething hard and the wooden rails were a problem. however, they’re not super thick, so I’m concerned about long-term wear. time will tell, I guess!

the crib rail guards have been great at preventing my toddler from chewing on the crib. installation was a breeze, but they started slipping after a couple of weeks. would prefer a tighter fit for better safety!
we've used these for around 6 weeks, and while they look cute and are soft, they don’t seem to stay in place. I’m constantly fixing them. love the idea, wish they worked better!
my baby has been teething and this rail cover has been a godsend! it protects the crib nicely, but cleaning is a hassle as it’s not machine washable. still, I appreciate the safety it provides.

these rail protectors are okay. they’re easy to install and look pretty good, but I wish they were thicker for more protection. my baby still managed to get a few bites in on the wood. so... not perfect!

just got these crib rail padding! they fit my crib perfectly and match the decor. however, they do not seem very thick, so I hope they last through my baby’s teething phase. can’t complain much so far!
I was hopeful for these crib rail covers since they look so cute! however, they shifted a lot during the night, waking me up thinking the baby was falling out. just not a fan of the slipping.
these crib teething guards are a decent buy! they haven’t frayed yet after 2 months, which is a plus. I love that they come in fun patterns! but the sticky strips don’t hold as well as I’d hoped.
installed these crib rail protectors last week and I already feel better! my baby was biting the wood, and now she’s happy. only downside is they’re too short for my crib ends. wish they were longer!

pretty satisfied with these rail covers. they add a nice touch to the crib and stop my toddler from chewing the wood. however, one side kept coming off, so I had to adjust it a couple of times. overall, good!
I had some concerns about these rail pads before buying, but they’ve turned out pretty well. they’re soft and look nice, but they do require frequent readjusting. it’s a little annoying, honestly.

these are good for protecting the crib but aren't very thick! I worry that they're not enough for my teething 1-year-old. they look nice though, and easy to install. not sure I’d buy again at this price.
we got these crib rail guards a few weeks ago, and they seem to be holding up. I love the softness, but I occasionally find them sliding down. they do their job, but not without some hassle.

I love the variety of colors available for these crib covers! they’re soft and do a great job of protecting the crib. however, the velcro strips could be stickier – I had to readjust them multiple times already.

just started using these crib rail protectors and they look great! they fit well but I noticed they’re not super thick, so I’m not sure how much protection they will give in the long run. fingers crossed for durability!
these crib rail pads are okay, but not the best. they fit well, but they don’t cover the whole rail, which makes me worried about my baby. my daughter likes chewing on them; just hope they hold up!
these worked well for my teething baby. they’re super soft and easy to clean, but I found they slip down a bit which is annoying. it’s a great product overall, but they could use a better grip!

got these to protect our crib, and I’m mostly happy! my baby loves chewing them, but I wish they were a bit thicker for better protection. good colors and easy to clean, so I might consider getting them again.

my two cats love the cat bed frame! the elevated design is great for keeping them cozy while still being able to see what's going on around them. it's been about a month since we got it, and the quality is holding up really well. only downside is the wood scratches a bit easily, so i have to be careful with their claws.

this mattress for cats is seriously impressive. the memory foam helps my senior cat with joint issues. however, it took a few days for the smell to dissipate. now that it's all good, she loves curling up in it. just wish it could be a bit bigger for my larger cat to join her!

the nightstand is really cute and has enough space for my cat's food and some toys. it does wobble a little if my cat jumps on it, which is annoying but manageable. perfect for keeping things neat and tidy. overall, good buy for cat owners who need a little extra storage.

it’s just okay. the headboard looks stylish, but after a month it started to squeak when my cats jumped on it. it's not a big deal, but it’s not the sturdy piece I expected. it does match my decor nicely though, so that’s a win!

this is such a great purchase! my cat loves lounging on it. the fabric is soft and easy to clean, which is a major plus. we’ve had it for about 6 weeks and it still looks brand new. the only thing is it could be a bit wider for my hefty kitty.

my cat has been sleeping on this mattress for 2 months, and honestly, it's been a hit! he seems to prefer it over the old blanket. good support for his back. however, it does attract cat hair like a magnet—so be prepared to clean it regularly.

the bed frame was easy to assemble, which I appreciated! but the instructions were a bit vague, and I had to figure out a couple of things on my own. my cat seems to love it, but I wish it had a bit more storage space underneath.

I really wanted to love this mattress for my cats. it’s super soft and they seem to enjoy it. but the size is on the smaller side, so my larger cat can't fit comfortably. it works well for my smaller one, though.

this headboard is adorable! my cat loves to perch on it while I read at night. it does the job for what I needed, but installation was a bit tricky. could definitely use better hardware. still, it’s super cute in the bedroom!

I ordered two nightstands and they look perfect next to my bed! they have enough space for all of my cat’s stuff too. but one of them had a slight scratch on it when it arrived, which was disappointing. overall, they function well.
the cat mattress fit my needs perfectly! it’s comfy and relatively durable. my only complaint is that it took a week to arrive, and I was excited to get it right away. now my cat sleeps like a baby!
I’m mixed about this cat bed frame. it’s cute and stylish, but the assembly was a nightmare. took me over an hour! once it's together, it's sturdy and my cat loves it. hope it lasts for a while!

the nightstand is fine but not what I expected. it looks pretty but is a bit wobbly—concerned it won't hold up long-term with my rambunctious kitty. functionally, it holds everything I need, so I’ll make do for now.

got this mattress for my cats about a month ago. it’s padded nicely, but the cats aren’t as into it as I thought they’d be. they still prefer the couch most days. surprisingly, it’s held up well to their scratching.

the elevated cat bed is a game changer! they love being able to look out the window. only downside is it doesn't have a removable cover, so cleaning is a bit of a hassle. construction quality is solid, though.
this headboard is chic but a little on the flimsy side. my cat loves to claw at it, and I'm worried it won't last long. it matches my decor beautifully, but I may have to reinforce it somehow.
I’m pretty happy with my cat's new mattress! it’s soft and fluffy, but the shipping took forever. my cat loves it now, but I was a bit annoyed waiting almost two weeks. still a decent buy overall.

the bed frame feels sturdy and looks nice in my bedroom. my cat seems to feel safe sleeping under it. however, there were some assembly issues, and I ended up with a spare part that I couldn’t find a place for.

I thought this nightstand would be good for holding my cat's toys, but it’s a bit smaller than I expected. it does have a cute design, so I’m keeping it, but I have to find another spot for the litter supplies.
the mattress is decent but not as thick as I hoped. my cat seems happy, but I wish it had more support. it's fine for now, but I might need to replace it in a year or so.
this headboard is perfect for my decor! however, I had a hard time with the installation; it could have been explained better. once together, it looks lovely and my cat loves to lounge on top!

got the nightstand last week, and it’s cute! holds my cat's food and some decor nicely. however, one drawer doesn't glide well, and it can be a bit annoying. it works for now, but I might replace it later.

my cats don’t seem super into this mattress yet. they might just need time to adjust. it’s soft and all, but they still prefer their old blanket. I’m hopeful they’ll come around—it looks great anyway!

the elevated cat bed looks super modern and my cat loves it! but I have to say, I wasn’t impressed with the quality. it came with a small dent which wasn’t worth the hassle of returning. still functional, though!
the bed frame is pretty solid and matches my decor nicely. my cat jumped on it right away, which was a good sign! but the assembly took a lot longer than it should have—definitely needed two people.

this cat nightstand is a decent piece. it fits perfectly beside my bed and holds all my cat's toys, but the finish could be better. there's a few tiny nicks here and there. overall, not bad for the price!

this baby sleep positioner has been a lifesaver for my 4-month-old. the wedge design really helps with reflux, giving me peace of mind at night. however, it is a bit bulky, so it takes up a lot of space in the crib. overall, it’s effective, but I wish it were a bit more compact.

we've been using this newborn sleep positioner for about 6 weeks now and it’s helped our little one sleep longer stretches. I love the breathable material, but the fact that it’s not machine washable is a bit annoying. still, I’d buy it again!

I really wanted to love this wedge pillow, but it hasn’t worked as well as I hoped for my 3-month-old. she still tends to roll over, and I worry about her safety. the quality seems good though, and it's easy to adjust. I think it depends on the baby’s sleeping habits.

the baby sleep positioner we got is pretty nice! it keeps our baby at a slight incline which has helped with her gas issues. the cover is soft and easy to wipe down, but honestly, it doesn’t fit snugly in the crib, so we have to be careful with her rolling.

after trying a few different sleep aids, I finally found this baby sleep positioner. my daughter sleeps so much better now! she's almost 5 months old and the incline really helps. just wish it came in more colors instead of plain white!

this wedge pillow was a recommended purchase on a parenting forum, and I’m glad I bought it. it really does help with my little one’s digestion. however, it is tricky to get the cover off for washing, which is a drawback. still, a solid product overall.

we’ve been using this sleep positioner for about a month, and it’s been a hit! our baby seems more comfortable, but I do wish it had a non-slip base. it slides around a bit when she moves, which makes me nervous.

I found this sleep positioner after struggling with my baby’s sleep for weeks. it’s helped him stay in a safe position without rolling over. the only downside is that it can get a bit warm, so we have to keep an eye on the temperature. overall, I’m happy with the purchase!

this infant sleep aid is really cute, and my baby seems to like it! the design is adorable and fits well in the crib. I just wish the instructions were clearer; it took me a while to figure out how to position it correctly.

the baby sleep positioner works wonders for my 2-month-old who had trouble with reflux. the slight incline has made a noticeable difference in her comfort. only downside is that it takes a while to dry after washing, but it’s definitely been worth it for us.

not a total win for us. the sleep positioner felt safe and secure, but my baby quickly outgrew it at 4 months. it’s a nice product but I wish it had a longer lifespan for the price. good for the short term, but that’s about it.

I bought this sleep positioner hoping to help my baby sleep through the night. at first, it seemed to work, but now at 5 months he’s learned to roll over. it’s not the product’s fault, but just know it might only last a few months if your baby is an early mover!

this wedge pillow is a good alternative to other positioners. it keeps my baby at a safe angle, but I do wish it came with a thicker cover. it’s slightly less comfy than I expected for long nights, but it does its job well enough.

I received this sleep positioner as a baby shower gift and it’s been great for my 3-month-old. she sleeps more soundly, and I appreciate the extra safety features. the only con is it’s a bit pricey compared to other options, but for us, it’s been worth it.

I was skeptical about sleep positioners, but this one has proven to be effective. my 6-month-old seems more settled at night. however, it’s quite big and takes up a lot of room in the crib. if you have a smaller crib, be cautious!

overall, I like this sleep positioner. it’s made my baby’s sleep much more comfortable, but it’s tricky to find the right angle when setting it up. once I figured it out, it worked like a charm, but it took some trial and error.
so far, this wedge pillow has really helped with my newborn's sleep. I love the ease of cleaning it, but I do wish the material were a bit softer. it seems to be sturdy and safe, which is a plus.

this baby sleep positioner has been a solid choice for us. it gives my baby the proper angle to help with her reflux. however, I’ve noticed that the assembly was not as straightforward as I hoped. only a few adjustments can make a difference, so just be careful!

we’ve had mixed results with this sleep positioner. my baby is more comfortable, and it seems safe, but I had a bit of trouble getting her to sleep on it initially. she prefers to roll around and wiggle, but it has definitely helped with her resting more peaceful.

I love this baby sleep aid! the incline truly helps my baby with her digestion, and she’s been sleeping longer stretches. it’s a little pricey, but I think it’s worth it for the quality. would recommend to other new parents!

my little one is more inclined to sleep soundly with this positioner, which is great. I wish it were easier to travel with since it’s a bit bulky. but as a stationary sleep aid, it’s been helpful for the last 2 months.

this was recommended by a friend, and I can see why. our baby sleeps much better now! only downside is the fabric isn’t as soft as I’d like for sensitive skin. other than that, it’s been a fantastic addition to our nightly routine.

these lentils cook up in under 30 minutes and are super versatile! i made a lentil soup last week that my kids devoured. but, they did give off a bit of foam while cooking, which was a little annoying. overall, pretty satisfied!

chickpeas were really fresh and plump, but i found that some of them were a bit hard compared to others. made a great batch of hummus but i had to sort through them first. will buy again just hope for more consistency!

the variety in this dried beans pack is awesome! had a family gathering and everyone loved the three-bean chili i made. the only downside? the cooking time was longer than expected—about 1.5 hours for some beans. but overall, very tasty!

love how these black beans are organic and free of additives. i soaked them overnight and they cooked really nicely. wish the packaging wasn't so big though—i end up with a lot left over, but hey, more chili for later!

these split peas were a great addition to my pantry. cooked them up for an easy soup, and it’s super filling! just a heads-up though, they can get mushy very quickly so keep an eye on them while cooking!

i was looking for some good quality navy beans, and i think i found them. they taste great in my bean salad! however, i wish they came in a smaller bag since it's just me and the leftovers are piling up in the pantry.

the lentils are okay, nothing special. they took a bit longer to cook than i expected—around 40 minutes. they work well in stews, but i was hoping for a softer texture. might try a different brand next time.

chickpeas are a pantry staple for me, especially for salads. these ones have a good taste, but the size varies a lot in the bag. some are super small while others are big! still worth it for the price, though—about $20 for 2lbs.

the variety in this pack is great, but the quality was hit or miss. the red beans were fantastic, but the black beans were a bit too hard even after soaking all night. definitely a mixed bag, pun intended!

i love these organic kidney beans! they’ve been great for my chili recipe. they cook evenly, but the only downside is that they take a while to soak. about 8 hours was best for me. otherwise, no complaints!

these dried peas are perfect for soups! they cook quickly and are super cheap, but when you open the bag there is quite a bit of dust. i rinsed them off, which helped, but just a little annoying. would buy again!

i tried these pinto beans for the first time, and they turned out pretty good. they took a bit longer to cook, almost 2 hours, but they were super flavorful in my burritos! just wish they'd soften a little faster!

just made a batch of lentil tacos using these green lentils, and they were fantastic! they held up well, but i did have to watch them closely to avoid mushiness. next time, i’ll try a shorter cooking time!

love the convenience of having a variety pack of beans on hand. used them for a bean salad and they added such great color! just be aware, the smaller sizes can burn easily if cooked too long. overall, still a great buy!
the chickpeas cooked evenly and made a delicious hummus, but I did find the package a bit bulky. might be better if they came in smaller sizes. still, decent value for the price!

the quality of these lentils is impressive! they cooked easily, but i found a few tiny stones in the bag. i’d recommend rinsing before cooking. other than that, they made an amazing dal that i’ve been craving!

had high hopes for these black-eyed peas, but they were not the best. took way longer to cook than the package said—over an hour! they turned out okay eventually but some felt a bit undercooked. not sure if i’ll buy again.

love using these kidney beans in my chili! they add such great texture and flavor, but man, the cooking time is a bit longer than i prefer. ended up taking about 1.5 hours to get them just right. worth it though!

the lentils were super easy to cook and made a great side dish. however, i did find some small rocks in the mix. make sure to sort before cooking! other than that, they added a nice protein boost to my meal!
these organic chickpeas are a must for hummus lovers! they blended so smoothly, but the package felt a bit small for the price. i might need to buy two bags next time. still, very tasty and fresh!

purchased these dried beans for some meal prep, and they were pretty good. however, the variety wasn’t what I expected; the lentils all ended up cooking faster than the larger beans. made for some uneven results!

worth the money if you’re into making stews! just a heads up, the cooking times can vary widely between different beans, so keep an eye on them. loved the flavor, but it was a bit of a pain managing the cooking times!
these red lentils were a game changer in my curries! they cooked up nice and creamy. only downside was that I had to rinse them thoroughly to remove any grit. but man, so worth the effort!

great selection of pulses, but the cooking instructions were not very clear. my first attempt ended up a little mushy. after some trial and error, i got them right, but it was a bit frustrating at first!

the chickpeas were decent, made a great salad, but they weren’t as tender as I hoped. I feel like they needed more soaking time. didn’t stop us from enjoying them though! just a little tougher than I expected.

this mustard is so tangy and flavorful! we’ve tried a ton of mustards, but this one has a nice kick that puts a twist on my sandwiches. however, it’s a bit thicker than what I’m used to, so I sometimes struggle to spread it evenly. still, it’s become a staple in our fridge.

the ketchup is decent, but honestly, it’s a little too sweet for my taste. I’ve been using it on fries and burgers for about a month now, and while it works well, I wish it had more of that classic tang. if you like sweeter ketchups, this might be for you.

I was really excited to try this relish after seeing so many positive reviews. it’s crunchy and has a nice vinegar bite, perfect for hot dogs! but I noticed there are a lot of spices that can overpower the taste if you’re not careful. still, it’s a fun addition to the condiment collection.

I’ve been a hot sauce lover for years, but this one fell a bit flat for me. sure, it has decent heat, but I was hoping for more flavor depth. I made tacos with it last week, and it worked okay, but I won’t be buying it again anytime soon. just didn’t wow me.

love this mustard! it’s a perfect balance of spicy and sweet. I’ve used it in potato salad and on sandwiches, and everyone raves about it. the only downside is the bottle is a bit hard to squeeze, so I end up using more than I intended. but I’ll keep buying it for sure!

ketchup is okay, nothing special honestly. we’ve been using it for the kids’ meals and sometimes on fries, but it feels a bit runny compared to others we’ve tried. I think we’ll finish the bottle but probably look for something thicker next time. just average.

this is the best relish I’ve ever had! I put it on everything from burgers to rice and it adds an amazing crunch. the only issue is that the jar doesn’t last very long. I found myself buying another one within two weeks. still, can’t get enough of it!

I got this hot sauce because I heard it was spicy, and wow, it definitely packs a punch! it’s great on eggs and tacos, but be careful, a little goes a long way. my only complaint is I wish it had a bit more flavor along with the heat, but overall, I’m happy with it.

this mustard is a staple in our house! I love using it for dipping pretzels and making salad dressings. it’s creamy and has just the right amount of zing. however, it does separate a bit in the fridge, so I have to shake it before each use, which is a minor hassle.

the ketchup is fine, but honestly, I expected more. it has a nice tomato taste, but it’s a bit too sweet for my liking. I’ve been using it for burgers and fries, and while it works, I think I’ll stick with my usual brand next time. it’s not bad, just not my favorite.

this relish is super tasty! it adds a nice crunch to my tuna salad sandwiches. the only downside is the jar is a bit small for the price, but I can’t help but go back for more because it’s so addictive. if you’re a relish fan, give it a shot.

very disappointed with this hot sauce. I really wanted to love it, but it lacks flavor and just seems overly vinegary. I tried it on chicken wings and it didn’t give them the kick I was hoping for. won't be buying again unless they change the recipe.

I’ve tried a lot of mustards, but this one stands out. the flavor is robust and pairs well with grilled meats. my only complaint is the bottle design; it can be messy pouring it out. still, I’ve used it for BBQs, and everyone loves it, so I’ll keep it on hand.

the ketchup is decent for an everyday sauce, but it could use a little more spice. we’ve been using it for burgers and fries for about a week, and while it satisfies the kids, it’s not something I’d reach for as an adult. pretty average in my opinion.

the relish is a little too sweet for my taste, but I’ve found it works well on hot dogs. I’ve been using it for the past month, and I think it’s just a matter of preference. if you like sweeter flavors, you’ll probably love it. for me, it’s just okay.

this hot sauce is amazing! I put it on everything from tacos to popcorn, and it’s the perfect amount of heat. my only gripe is the bottle can get sticky after frequent use. otherwise, it’s my go-to, and I’ve bought it three times already! definitely a keeper.

I was excited to try this mustard, but it’s just okay. it has a nice flavor, but I didn’t find it to be all that different from regular mustards. I used it for burgers and sandwiches, but I likely won’t repurchase. it’s not bad, just not worth the hype for me.

this ketchup is a bit better than your standard bottle. there’s a hint of spice that makes it unique. however, I wish it came in a bigger size because my family goes through it so fast! overall, a solid addition to our pantry.

the relish is tangy and zesty, making it a great topping for burgers. however, I found it to be a bit too salty for my taste. a little goes a long way, but if you don’t mind the saltiness, it’s a solid option. been using it for about a month!

this hot sauce isn’t too spicy, which works well for my kids, but I find myself reaching for something else when I want a kick. it has a nice flavor, though, and I’ve used it in cooking. just wish it had more heat. probably won’t buy again unless they come out with a spicier version.

definitely loving this mustard! it’s got a great texture and flavor that adds so much to my sandwiches. however, the packaging could be improved as it’s a bit hard to squeeze out. despite that small issue, it's still my favorite mustard!

ketchup is okay at best. we tried it on burgers and fries, but it didn’t stand out like I hoped. kind of bland honestly. not awful, but I’ll be going back to my usual brand for sure. it’s just not memorable enough for me.

I’ve been using this relish for a while now, and it's grown on me. initially, I thought it was too sweet, but it’s perfect for adding flavor to sandwiches. just wish they offered a spicier version for those who crave a little heat.

the hot sauce is fine, not too spicy, just right for everyday use. I've been adding it to soups and marinades. however, I do wish it had more depth in flavor. it works for now, but I might keep looking for something with a bit more character.

This temporary tattoo ink is fantastic! I used it for a friend's birthday party and everyone loved it. It stayed on for about a week and didn't smudge at all. One downside is that it takes a bit longer to dry than I expected, so maybe give it an hour before you get too active. Overall, great for parties!

I tried these henna tattoos for the first time and I loved the designs! They looked so intricate and pretty. However, I found that they didn't last as long as I hoped—about 4 days before they started to fade. Still, it's a fun way to try body art without commitment!

The quality of this tattoo ink is decent, but I wish the colors were more vibrant. I did a small design on my arm, and while it looked good at first, it faded pretty quickly, like within a week. But it was super easy to apply, so that’s a plus!

Got this tattoo ink for a cosplay event, and it worked well for my needs. The application was straightforward, and it didn’t irritate my sensitive skin. But, I did notice some parts started to wear off after just a few hours of dancing. Just keep that in mind if you plan on being active!

These temporary tattoos are a hit with my kids! They love picking out designs and applying them. They last about 3-5 days, which is perfect for our weekend outings. Just be careful when applying, as the adhesive can sometimes leave a sticky residue. But, overall, a fun product!

I’ve used various brands of henna, but this one stood out because of its creaminess. It’s easy to work with and the color turns out really rich. Unfortunately, I did experience some skin irritation after a few hours, which was disappointing. Wouldn’t recommend for sensitive skin types.

I've been using this body piercings kit, and it's super helpful for maintenance! The tools are nice and sharp, which makes the cleaning process easy. The downside? It could use a bit more guidance for beginners—some techniques took a bit of trial and error.

Not crazy about this tattoo ink. I expected it to be longer lasting based on reviews, but mine started fading after just a few days. It applied nicely, but I was hoping for more durability. Maybe it works better for smaller designs!

The selection of designs in this temporary tattoo pack is amazing! I had a blast choosing different pieces for my beach trip. However, some tattoos were a bit tricky to peel off after applying; I ended up tearing a few. They still looked great though!

I love this henna kit! The instructions were super clear, and I could achieve beautiful results on my hands. However, be prepared; the smell of the paste was a little strong for my liking. Still, it’s definitely worth trying if you're into DIY body art!

Honestly, I was skeptical about this temp tattoo ink, but I was pleasantly surprised. It held up for about a week and came off easily when I was ready. Just make sure to apply it carefully and let it dry properly. A fun experience, for sure!

I got some cool designs from this temporary tattoo set for a party. They went on easily and looked great, but the downside was that they started to peel at the edges after a few days. Still, for a short-term decoration, I think it’s worth it!

This tattoo ink is okay, but it really needs to improve the color payoff. I did a small floral design, and the color was way too light for my liking. It took several layers to make it pop, which was frustrating. Application was good though, so there's that!

Bought these henna cones and was totally amazed by how vibrant the colors turned out! I did a full arm design for a festival, and it looked stunning. Just keep in mind that it takes some time to achieve a deep color, so plan ahead!

I really wanted to love this body piercing starter kit, but it had some issues. The needles were fine, but the aftercare instructions were hard to follow for a newbie like me. I ended up searching for tutorials online, which was frustrating!

The temporary tattoos are a fun way to express creativity! I designed a whole arm sleeve for a costume party, which turned out great. The only thing I didn’t like was that some tattoos got a bit wrinkled during application, but they still looked awesome!

I used this tattoo ink for a small design on my wrist. The application was a breeze, and it dried pretty quickly. However, it did wear off after about 5 days, and I wish it was more durable. Perfect for a short-term look though!

These henna tattoos were a cool addition to our festival look! Although the designs were beautiful, they didn’t last as long as expected—about 3 days. Would’ve loved for them to stand the test of time a bit longer, but it was still a fun experience!

Love the variety of colors in this body art kit! It really gives options for when you're feeling creative. However, I found some colors didn’t show up well on deeper skin tones, which was a bummer. Still, I loved the versatility!

Tried this temporary tattoo set for a family event, and it was a big hit! The tattoos were easy to apply, and everyone had fun picking their favorites. But, I wish the pack included more designs—felt a bit limited after a while. Overall, a solid choice!

This tattoo ink was a game changer for my last-minute Halloween costume! It applied easily and the tips were precise for details. My only complaint was that it smudged a bit after a few hours, so if you’re planning to sweat, beware!

The quality of this henna is really nice. It’s easy to work with and produces rich designs. That said, I experienced a little staining on my skin after I washed it off, which surprised me. But the designs were totally worth it!

Purchased this temporary tattoo ink for a child's birthday party, and it was a blast! All the kids loved seeing their designs come to life. One downside, however, was that some tattoos peeled off faster than others. But overall, a fun activity!
I really like this body piercing kit! It has everything you need to take care of your piercings, but I wish it came with a guide for proper usage. Would’ve saved me some time researching online!

These henna cones are pretty good! They had a nice consistency and were fairly easy to apply. Do note, though, that they take some patience to dry completely. A bit of a wait, but the results are lovely once they take!

The tattoo ink worked pretty well for my needs. It was easy to apply and didn’t irritate my skin. But it did fade faster than I anticipated—like, within about 4 days. Still, fun for short-term use and easy to remove when I wanted!

this multi-surface cleaner has been a lifesaver for my kitchen! it cuts through grease like nobody's business. my only issue is the scent – it's a bit strong for my liking, but it does the job. I've been using it for about a month now, and my countertops have never looked cleaner!

the all-purpose cleaner is decent, but it sometimes leaves a residue on my glass surfaces. I've had it for a few weeks, and while it works well on counters, I wouldn’t use it on windows again. maybe I should try their glass-specific cleaner next time?

I've been using this disinfectant spray for a couple of months, and it really does give me peace of mind. I love that it kills 99.9% of germs, especially with kids around. however, it dries pretty quickly, so I have to work fast to wipe it away. overall, a solid buy.

super impressed with the streak-free glass cleaner! I’ve tried others that just left my windows smeary, but this one did the trick. I could see clearly after just one wipe. my only complaint is the bottle is a bit small for the price. but hey, it works!

this surface cleaner smells amazing and is safe to use around my dogs! I appreciate that. I’ve been using it for about 6 weeks now, and it does a good job on my counters and tables. just wish it came in a bigger size to save a few bucks!

the disinfectant wipes are convenient but not the best for heavy-duty cleaning. I carry them in my diaper bag for quick clean-ups, but they don't remove stuck-on messes all that well. still, it’s nice to have them around.

I wanted to love this all-purpose cleaner because of the eco-friendly label, but honestly, it doesn’t cut through stains as well as I'd hoped. it smells great, but I end up scrubbing a lot more. probably won't repurchase.

this is one of the better glass cleaners I've used! no streaks and it dries almost instantly. however, I do find that it’s a bit pricey for the amount you get, so I’m mixing it with some water to stretch it out a bit.

we've been using this surface cleaner for a few months now, and it’s been great for everyday spills and messes. I love that it’s natural, but I wish it had a stronger scent! sometimes it feels like it’s not really cleaning especially tough stains.

picked up this disinfectant spray when the kids were sick. it works great and has a nice, fresh scent. I feel safer knowing I’m sanitizing surfaces. just wish the spray nozzle was a bit finer – it tends to make a mess sometimes.

the all-purpose cleaner does a decent job, but I found I have to use quite a bit to really get rid of tougher grime. it’s nice that it’s safe for different surfaces, and I appreciate it being biodegradable. just a little disappointed in the scrubbing power.
